               found_inf=getattr(self, \"found_inf\", None),\n                decoupled_weight_decay=group[\"decoupled_weight_decay\"],\n            )\n\n        return loss\n\n\nAdam.__doc__ = (\n    r\"\"\"Implements Adam algorithm.\n\n    .. math::\n       \\begin{aligned}\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma \\text{ (lr)}, \\beta_1, \\beta_2\n                \\text{ (betas)},\\theta_0 \\text{ (params)},f(\\theta) \\text{ (objective)}          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{13mm}      \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)},  \\: \\textit{amsgrad},\n                \\:\\textit{maximize},  \\: \\epsilon \\text{ (epsilon)}                              \\\\\n            &\\textbf{initialize} :  m_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ ( first moment)},\n                v_0\\leftarrow 0 \\text{ (second moment)},\\: v_0^{max}\\leftarrow 0          \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{maximize}:                                       \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   -\\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})         \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{else}                                                           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda  \\theta_{t-1}                            \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}m_t           \\leftarrow   \\beta_1 m_{t-1} + (1 - \\beta_1) g_t          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}v_t           \\leftarrow   \\beta_2 v_{t-1} + (1-\\beta_2) g^2_t          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\widehat{m_t} \\leftarrow   m_t/\\big(1-\\beta_1^t \\big)                   \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: amsgrad                                                  \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm} v_t^{max} \\leftarrow \\mathrm{max}(v_{t-1}^{max},v_t)                  \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}\\widehat{v_t} \\leftarrow v_t^{max}/\\big(1-\\beta_2^t \\big)              \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{else}                                                           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}\\widehat{v_t} \\leftarrow   v_t/\\big(1-\\beta_2^t \\big)                  \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1} - \\gamma \\widehat{m_t}/\n                \\big(\\sqrt{\\widehat{v_t}} + \\epsilon \\big)                                       \\\\\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n       \\end{aligned}\n\n    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to `Adam: A Method for Stochastic Optimization`_.\n    \"\"\"\n    + rf\"\"\"\n    Args:\n        {_params_doc}\n        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-3). A tensor LR\n            is not yet supported for all our implementations. Please use a float\n            LR if you are not also specifying fused=True or capturable=True.\n        betas (Tuple[float, float], optional): coefficients used for computing\n            running averages of gradient and its square (default: (0.9, 0.999))\n        eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve\n            numerical stability (default: 1e-8)\n        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n        decoupled_weight_decay (bool, optional): if True, this optimizer is\n            equivalent to AdamW and the algorithm will not accumulate weight\n            decay in the momentum nor variance. (default: False)\n        amsgrad (bool, optional): whether to use the AMSGrad variant of this\n            algorithm from the paper `On the Convergence of Adam and Beyond`_\n            (default: False)\n        {_foreach_doc}\n        {_maximize_doc}\n        {_capturable_doc}\n        {_differentiable_doc}\n        {_fused_doc}\n    .. Note::\n        A prototype implementation of Adam and AdamW for MPS supports `torch.float32` and `torch.float16`.\n    .. _Adam\\: A Method for Stochastic Optimization:\n        https://arxiv.org/abs/1412.6980\n    .. _On the Convergence of Adam and Beyond:\n        https://openreview.net/forum?id=ryQu7f-RZ\n\n    \"\"\"\n)\n\n\ndef _single_tensor_adam(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n    max_exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    amsgrad: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n    beta1: Union[float, Tensor],\n    beta2: Union[float, Tensor],\n    lr: Union[float, Tensor],\n    weight_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    capturable: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    decoupled_weight_decay: bool,\n):\n    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n\n    if torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        # this assert is due to JIT being dumb and not realizing that the ops below\n        # have overloads to handle both float and Tensor lrs, so we just assert it's\n        # a float since most people using JIT are using floats\n        assert isinstance(lr, float)\n        assert isinstance(beta1, float)\n        assert isinstance(beta2, float)\n    else:\n        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n        # TODO: Support nonzero-dim Tensor betas, see #147921\n\n    # We only shuffle around the beta when it is a Tensor, otherwise, we prefer\n    # treating it as a scalar.\n    # Note: ensure type declaration is under conditional check for isinstance\n    # or else torchscript will get cranky about the DeviceDict type.\n    if isinstance(beta1, Tensor):\n        beta1_dict: Optional[DeviceDtypeDict] = {(beta1.device, beta1.dtype): beta1}\n    else:\n        beta1_dict = None\n\n    for i, param in enumerate(params):\n        grad = grads[i] if not maximize else -grads[i]\n        exp_avg = exp_avgs[i]\n        exp_avg_sq = exp_avg_sqs[i]\n        step_t = state_steps[i]\n\n        # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n            capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices()\n            assert (\n                param.device.type == step_t.device.type\n                and param.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n            ), (\n                f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n            )\n\n        # update step\n        step_t += 1\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            if decoupled_weight_decay:\n                # Perform stepweight decay\n                param.mul_(1 - lr * weight_decay)\n            else:\n                # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules\n                if differentiable and isinstance(weight_decay, Tensor):\n                    if weight_decay.requires_grad:\n                        grad = grad.addcmul_(param.clone(), weight_decay)\n                    else:\n                        grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n                else:\n                    grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n\n        if torch.is_complex(param):\n            grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n            exp_avg = torch.view_as_real(exp_avg)\n            exp_avg_sq = torch.view_as_real(exp_avg_sq)\n            if amsgrad:\n                max_exp_avg_sqs[i] = torch.view_as_real(max_exp_avg_sqs[i])\n            param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n\n        device = param.device\n\n        if beta1_dict is not None:\n            dtype = param.dtype  # type: ignore[union-attr]\n\n            # cast to workaround https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/140601\n            key = (device, dtype)\n            if key not in beta1_dict:\n                beta1_dict[key] = beta1.to(  # type: ignore[union-attr]\n                    device=device, dtype=dtype, non_blocking=True\n                )\n\n            device_beta1: Union[float, Tensor] = beta1_dict[key]\n        else:\n            device_beta1 = beta1\n\n        # Decay the first and second moment running average coefficient\n        exp_avg.lerp_(grad, 1 - device_beta1)\n\n        # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules\n        if differentiable and isinstance(beta2, Tensor):\n            if beta2.requires_grad:\n                # Using lerp to only use 2 operations bc addcmul's value cannot be a tensor\n                # Showing equivalence of differentiable path and nondifferentiable path\n                # expavg * b2 + grad^2 * (1-b2)\n                #           add expavg * (1-b2) - expavg * (1-b2) = 0\n                # expavg * b2 + expavg * (1-b2) - expavg * (1-b2) + grad^2 * (1-b2)\n                # expavg - expavg * (1-b2) + grad^2 * (1-b2)\n                # expavg + (grad^2 - expavg) * (1-b2)\n                # expavg.lerp(grad^2, 1-beta2)\n                exp_avg_sq.lerp_(torch.square(grad), weight=1 - beta2)\n            else:\n                exp_avg_sq.mul_(beta2).addcmul_(\n                    grad, grad, value=cast(float, 1 - beta2)\n                )\n        else:\n            exp_avg_sq.mul_(beta2).addcmul_(grad, grad, value=1 - beta2)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n\n        if capturable or differentiable:\n            step = step_t\n\n            # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules\n            if differentiable and isinstance(beta1, Tensor):\n                if beta1.requires_grad:\n                    bias_correction1 = 1 - beta1 ** step.clone()\n                else:\n                    bias_correction1 = 1 - beta1**step\n            else:\n                bias_correction1 = 1 - beta1**step\n\n            # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules\n            if differentiable and isinstance(beta2, Tensor):\n                if beta2.requires_grad:\n                    bias_correction2 = 1 - beta2 ** step.clone()\n                else:\n                    bias_correction2 = 1 - beta2**step\n            else:\n                bias_correction2 = 1 - beta2**step\n\n            step_size = lr / bias_correction1\n            step_size_neg = step_size.neg()\n\n            bias_correction2_sqrt = bias_correction2.sqrt()\n\n            if amsgrad:\n                # Maintains the maximum of all 2nd moment running avg. till now\n                if differentiable:\n                    max_exp_avg_sq = max_exp_avg_sqs[i].clone()\n                else:\n                    max_exp_avg_sq = max_exp_avg_sqs[i]\n\n                max_exp_avg_sqs[i].copy_(torch.maximum(max_exp_avg_sq, exp_avg_sq))\n\n                # Uses the max. for normalizing running avg. of gradient\n                # Folds in (admittedly ugly) 1-elem step_size math here to avoid extra param-set-sized read+write\n                # (can't fold it into addcdiv_ below because addcdiv_ requires value is a Number, not a Tensor)\n                denom = (\n                    max_exp_avg_sqs[i].sqrt() / (bias_correction2_sqrt * step_size_neg)\n                ).add_(eps / step_size_neg)\n            else:\n                denom = (\n                    exp_avg_sq.sqrt() / (bias_correction2_sqrt * step_size_neg)\n                ).add_(eps / step_size_neg)\n\n            if differentiable:\n                param.addcdiv_(exp_avg.clone(), denom)\n            else:\n                param.addcdiv_(exp_avg, denom)\n        else:\n            step = _get_value(step_t)\n\n            bias_correction1 = 1 - beta1**step\n            bias_correction2 = 1 - beta2**step\n\n            step_size = lr / bias_correction1\n\n            bias_correction2_sqrt = bias_correction2**0.5\n\n            if amsgrad:\n                # Maintains the maximum of all 2nd moment running avg. till now\n                torch.maximum(max_exp_avg_sqs[i], exp_avg_sq, out=max_exp_avg_sqs[i])\n\n                # Use the max. for normalizing running avg. of gradient\n                denom = (max_exp_avg_sqs[i].sqrt() / bias_correction2_sqrt).add_(eps)\n            else:\n                denom = (exp_avg_sq.sqrt() / bias_correction2_sqrt).add_(eps)\n\n            param.addcdiv_(exp_avg, denom, value=-step_size)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n\n        # Lastly, switch back to complex view\n        if amsgrad and torch.is_complex(params[i]):\n            max_exp_avg_sqs[i] = torch.view_as_complex(max_exp_avg_sqs[i])\n\n\ndef _multi_tensor_adam(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n    max_exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    amsgrad: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n    beta1: Union[float, Tensor],\n    beta2: Union[float, Tensor],\n    lr: Union[float, Tensor],\n    weight_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    capturable: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    decoupled_weight_decay: bool,\n):\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    if isinstance(lr, Tensor):\n        if not capturable:\n            raise RuntimeError(\n                \"lr as a Tensor is not supported for capturable=False and foreach=True\"\n            )\n        if lr.numel() != 1:\n            raise ValueError(\"Tensor lr must be 1-element\")\n\n    if isinstance(beta1, Tensor):\n        if not capturable:\n            raise ValueError(\n                \"beta1 as a Tensor is not supported for capturable=False and foreach=True\"\n            )\n        if beta1.numel() != 1:\n            raise ValueError(\"Tensor beta1 must be 1-element\")\n\n    if isinstance(beta2, Tensor):\n        if not capturable:\n            raise ValueError(\n                \"beta2 as a Tensor is not supported for capturable=False and foreach=True\"\n            )\n        if beta2.numel() != 1:\n            raise ValueError(\"Tensor beta2 must be 1-element\")\n\n    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(\n            supports_xla=False\n        )\n        assert all(\n            p.device.type == step.device.type\n            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n            for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)\n        ), (\n            f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n        )\n\n    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n\n    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n    # TODO: Support nonzero-dim Tensor betas, see #147921\n\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, exp_avgs, exp_avg_sqs, max_exp_avg_sqs, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n\n    # We only shuffle around the beta when it is a Tensor and on CUDA, otherwise, we prefer\n    # treating it as a scalar.\n    beta1_dict: Optional[DeviceDict] = (  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n        {beta1.device: beta1}\n        if isinstance(beta1, Tensor) and str(beta1.device) != \"cpu\"\n        else None\n    )\n\n    for (\n        device_params_,\n        device_grads_,\n        device_exp_avgs_,\n        device_exp_avg_sqs_,\n        device_max_exp_avg_sqs_,\n        device_state_steps_,\n    ), _ in grouped_tensors.values():\n        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n        device_exp_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], device_exp_avgs_)\n        device_exp_avg_sqs = cast(list[Tensor], device_exp_avg_sqs_)\n        device_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_steps_)\n\n        device = device_params[0].device\n        if beta1_dict is not None and device not in beta1_dict:\n            beta1_dict[device] = beta1.to(device=device, non_blocking=True)  # type: ignore[union-attr, attr-defined]\n\n        device_beta1 = beta1_dict[device] if beta1_dict else beta1\n\n        # Handle complex parameters\n        if has_complex:\n            if amsgrad:\n                device_max_exp_avg_sqs = cast(list[Tensor], device_max_exp_avg_sqs_)\n                _view_as_real(\n                    device_params,\n                    device_grads,\n                    device_exp_avgs,\n                    device_exp_avg_sqs,\n                    device_max_exp_avg_sqs,\n                )\n            else:\n                _view_as_real(\n                    device_params, device_grads, device_exp_avgs, device_exp_avg_sqs\n                )\n\n        if maximize:\n            device_grads = torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        # Update steps\n        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and device_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n            torch._foreach_add_(\n                device_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n            )\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            if decoupled_weight_decay:\n                # Perform stepweight decay\n                torch._foreach_mul_(device_params, 1 - lr * weight_decay)\n            else:\n                # Reuse the intermediate memory (device_grads) already allocated for maximize\n                if maximize:\n                    torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n                else:\n                    device_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n                        device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay\n                    )\n\n        # Decay the first and second moment running average coefficient\n        # Use device beta1 if beta1 is a tensor to ensure all\n        # tensors are on the same device\n        torch._foreach_lerp_(\n            device_exp_avgs, device_grads, cast(float, 1 - device_beta1)\n        )\n\n        torch._foreach_mul_(device_exp_avg_sqs, beta2)\n\n        # Due to the strictness of the _foreach_addcmul API, we can't have a single\n        # tensor scalar as the scalar arg (only python number is supported there)\n        # as a result, separate out the value mul\n        # Filed https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/139795\n        if isinstance(beta2, torch.Tensor):\n            scaled_device_grads = torch._foreach_mul(device_grads, 1 - beta2)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n            value = 1.0\n        else:\n            scaled_device_grads = device_grads  # type: ignore[assignment]\n            value = 1 - beta2\n\n        torch._foreach_addcmul_(\n            device_exp_avg_sqs, scaled_device_grads, device_grads, value\n        )\n\n        # Delete the local intermediate(s) since they won't be used anymore to save on peak memory\n        del device_grads\n        del scaled_device_grads\n\n        bias_correction1: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n        bias_correction2: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n        bias_correction2_sqrt: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n\n        if capturable:\n            bias_correction1 = torch._foreach_pow(beta1, device_state_steps)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n            bias_correction2 = torch._foreach_pow(beta2, device_state_steps)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n            # foreach_sub doesn't allow a scalar as the first arg\n            torch._foreach_sub_(bias_correction1, 1)\n            torch._foreach_sub_(bias_correction2, 1)\n            # we do not negate bias_correction1 as it'll need to be negated later anyway\n            torch._foreach_neg_(bias_correction2)\n\n            # foreach_div doesn't allow a scalar as the first arg\n            torch._foreach_div_(bias_correction1, lr)\n            torch._foreach_reciprocal_(bias_correction1)\n\n            torch._foreach_sqrt_(bias_correction2)\n\n            # Re-assign for clarity as we maintain minimal intermediates: we'll have\n            # step_size = - lr / (1 - beta1 ^ t) where t = num_steps\n            # bias_correction2_sqrt = sqrt(1 - beta2 ^ t)\n            step_size = bias_correction1\n            bias_correction2_sqrt = bias_correction2\n\n            if amsgrad:\n                device_max_exp_avg_sqs = cast(list[Tensor], device_max_exp_avg_sqs_)\n                # Maintains the maximum of all 2nd moment running avg. till now\n                torch._foreach_maximum_(device_max_exp_avg_sqs, device_exp_avg_sqs)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n                # Set intermediate to the max. for normalizing running avg. of gradient when amsgrad\n                exp_avg_sq_sqrt = torch._foreach_sqrt(device_max_exp_avg_sqs)\n            else:\n                exp_avg_sq_sqrt = torch._foreach_sqrt(device_exp_avg_sqs)\n\n            torch._foreach_div_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, bias_correction2_sqrt)\n            torch._foreach_add_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, eps)\n            torch._foreach_div_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, step_size)\n\n            # at this point, exp_avg_sq_sqrt = - (1 - beta^t) * [sqrt(exp_avg_sq / (1 - beta2^t)) + eps] / lr\n            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(device_params, device_exp_avgs, exp_avg_sq_sqrt)\n        else:\n            bias_correction1 = [\n                1 - beta1 ** _get_value(step) for step in device_state_steps\n            ]\n            bias_correction2 = [\n                1 - beta2 ** _get_value(step) for step in device_state_steps\n            ]\n\n            step_size = _stack_if_compiling([(lr / bc) * -1 for bc in bias_correction1])\n\n            bias_correction2_sqrt = [bc**0.5 for bc in bias_correction2]  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n\n            if amsgrad:\n                device_max_exp_avg_sqs = cast(list[Tensor], device_max_exp_avg_sqs_)\n                # Maintains the maximum of all 2nd moment running avg. till now\n                torch._foreach_maximum_(device_max_exp_avg_sqs, device_exp_avg_sqs)\n\n                # Use the max. for normalizing running avg. of gradient\n                exp_avg_sq_sqrt = torch._foreach_sqrt(device_max_exp_avg_sqs)\n            else:\n                exp_avg_sq_sqrt = torch._foreach_sqrt(device_exp_avg_sqs)\n\n            torch._foreach_div_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, bias_correction2_sqrt)\n            torch._foreach_add_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, eps)\n            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(\n                device_params,\n                device_exp_avgs,\n                exp_avg_sq_sqrt,\n                step_size,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n            )\n\n\ndef _fused_adam(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n    max_exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    amsgrad: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,  # Needed for consistency.\n    beta1: float,\n    beta2: float,\n    lr: Union[float, Tensor],\n    weight_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    capturable: bool,  # Needed for consistency.\n    differentiable: bool,\n    decoupled_weight_decay: bool,\n) -> None:\n    if not params:\n        return\n    if differentiable:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"Adam with fused=True does not support differentiable=True\")\n\n    grad_scale_dict: DeviceDict = (\n        {grad_scale.device: grad_scale} if grad_scale is not None else {}\n    )\n    found_inf_dict: DeviceDict = (\n        {found_inf.device: found_inf} if found_inf is not None else {}\n    )\n\n    # type: ignore[union-attr]\n            lr = lr_dict[device]\n        torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n        func = torch._fused_adam_ if not decoupled_weight_decay else torch._fused_adamw_\n        func(\n            device_params,\n            device_grads,\n            device_exp_avgs,\n            device_exp_avg_sqs,\n            device_max_exp_avg_sqs,  # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n    # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n    capturable: bool = False,\n    differentiable: bool = False,\n    fused: Optional[bool] = None,\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n    has_complex: bool = False,\n    decoupled_weight_decay: bool = False,\n    *,\n    amsgrad: bool,\n    beta1: float,\n    beta2: float,\n    lr: Union[float, Tensor],\n    weight_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n):\n    r\"\"\"Functional API that performs Adam algorithm computation.\n\n    See :class:`~torch.optim.Adam` for details.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Respect when the user inputs False/True for foreach or fused. We only want to change\n    # the default when neither have been user-specified. Note that we default to foreach\n    # and pass False to use_fused. This is not a mistake--we want to give the fused impl\n    # bake-in time before making it the default, even if it is typically faster.\n    if fused is None and foreach is None:\n        _, foreach = _default_to_fused_or_foreach(\n            params, differentiable, use_fused=False\n        )\n        # Do not flip on foreach for the unsupported case where lr is a Tensor and capturable=False.\n        if foreach and isinstance(lr, Tensor) and not capturable:\n            foreach = False\n    if fused is None:\n        fused = False\n    if foreach is None:\n        foreach = False\n\n    # r\"\"\"Implements AdamW algorithm, where weight decay does not accumulate in the momentum nor variance.\n    63→\n    64→    .. math::\n    65→       \\begin{aligned}\n    66→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n    67→            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma \\text{(lr)}, \\: \\beta_1, \\beta_2\n    68→                \\text{(betas)}, \\: \\theta_0 \\text{(params)}, \\: f(\\theta) \\text{(objective)},\n    69→                \\: \\epsilon \\text{ (epsilon)}          \\\\\n    72→            &\\textbf{initialize} : m_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ (first moment)}, v_0 \\leftarrow 0\n    73→                \\text{ ( second moment)}, \\: v_0^{max}\\leftarrow 0                        \\\\[-1.ex]\n    74→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n    75→            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: These algorithms are manually validated by comparing to the paper\n          and systematically validated by assuring that the loss goes the right direction\n          when the optimizer has been applied.\n        * This implementation should compose with optimizer hyperparameters well, such as\n          supporting Tensor LRs, the capturable API, and sparse and complex parameters.\n    - Each varying implementation\n        * We then have implementations that improve upon the performance of the forloop\n          implementation by leveraging fusion, namely our foreach (mult_tensor) and fused\n          implementations.\n        * These variations are validated numerically by comparing with the forloop version\n          of the optimizer. In fact, we test most variations this way--we see the forloop\n          implementation as the ground truth and expect that improvements to it in any way\n          should be just as correct.\n        * Both params and optimizer states should be validated numerically.\n    - state_dict APIs\n        * The optimizer instance should be serializable\n        * Calling save and load should be deterministic\n        * Moving between devices should be seamless\n        * BC - load_state_dict should be able to handle older optimizer states\n    - Hook APIs (everything should fire in the right order)\n    - LR Scheduler integration (composing should not error + should go the right direction)\n    - Parameter groups (should be equivalent to having multiple optimizers)\n    - Erroring (what should error should error)\n","numLines":100,"startLine":1,"totalLines":2252}}}

\\begin{aligned}\n   158→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   159→            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma \\text{ (lr)}, \\: \\theta_0 \\text{ (params)}, \\: f(\\theta)\n   160→                \\text{ (objective)}, \\: \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)},                          \\\\\n   161→            &\\hspace{13mm} &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   164→            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n   165→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{maximize}:                                       \\\\\n   166→            &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   -\\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})         \\\\\n   167→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{else}                                                           \\\\\n   168→            &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})          \\\\\n   169→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                           \\\\\n   170→            &\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda  \\theta_{t-1}                            \\\\\n   171→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\mu \\neq 0                                               \\\\\n   172→            &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{if} \\: t > 1                                                   \\\\\n   173→            &\\hspace{15mm} \\textbf{b}_t \\leftarrow \\mu \\textbf{b}_{t-1} + (1-\\tau) g_t           \\\\\n   174→            &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{else}                                                          \\\\\n   175→            &\\hspace{15mm} \\textbf{b}_t \\leftarrow g_t                                           \\\\\n   176→            &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{nesterov}                                       \\\\\n   177→            &\\hspace{15mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_{t} + \\mu \\textbf{b}_t                               \\\\\n   178→            &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{else}                                                   \\\\[-1.ex]\n   179→            &\\hspace{15mm} g_t  \\leftarrow  \\textbf{b}_t                                         \\\\\n   180→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1} - \\gamma g_t                    \\\\[-1.ex]\n   181→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   182→            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n   183→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   184→       \\end{aligned}\n   185→\n   186→    Nesterov momentum is based on the formula from\n   187→    `On the importance of initialization and momentum in deep learning`__.\n   188→    \"\"\"\n   189→    + rf\"\"\"\n   190→    Args:\n   191→        {_params_doc}\n   192→        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-3)\n   193→        momentum (float, (default: 0)\n   196→        nesterov (bool, optional): enables Nesterov momentum. Only applicable\n   197→            when momentum is non-zero. (default: False)\n   198→        {_maximize_doc}\n   199→        {_foreach_doc}\n   200→        {_differentiable_doc}\n   201→        {_fused_doc}\n   202→    \"\"\"\n   203→    + r\"\"\"\n   204→\n   205→    Example:\n   206→        >>> # xdoctest: +SKIP\n   207→        >>> optimizer = torch.optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.1, momentum=0.9)\n   208→        >>> optimizer.zero_grad()\n   209→        >>> loss_fn(model(input), target).backward()\n   210→        >>> optimizer.step()\n   211→\n   212→    __ http://www.cs.toronto.edu/%7Ehinton/absps/momentum.pdf\n   213→\n   214→    .. note::\n   215→        The implementation of SGD with Momentum/Nesterov subtly differs from\n   216→        Sutskever et al. and implementations in some other frameworks.\n   217→\n   218→        Considering the specific case of Momentum, the update can be written as\n   219→\n   220→        .. math::\n   221→            \\begin{aligned}\n   222→                v_{t+1} & = \\mu * v_{t} + g_{t+1}, \\\\\n   223→                p_{t+1} & = p_{t} - \\text{lr} * v_{t+1},\n   224→            \\end{aligned}\n   225→\n   226→        where :math:`p`, :math:`g`, :math:`v` and :math:`\\mu` denote the\n   227→        parameters, gradient, velocity, and momentum respectively.\n   228→\n   229→        This is in contrast to Sutskever et al. and\n   230→        other frameworks which employ an update of the form\n   231→\n   232→        .. math::\n   233→            \\begin{aligned}\n   234→                v_{t+1} & = \\mu * v_{t} + \\text{lr} * g_{t+1}, \\\\\n   235→                p_{t+1} & = p_{t} - v_{t+1}.\n   236→            \\end{aligned}\n   237→\n   238→        The Nesterov version is analogously modified.\n   239→\n   240→        Moreover, the initial value of the momentum buffer is set to the\n   241→        gradient value at the first step. This is in contrast to some other\n   242→        frameworks that initialize it to all zeros. One notable side effect\n   243→        of this decision is that the first momentum value will not be scaled\n   244→        by dampening. Dampening will be applied starting at the second step.\n   245→\n   246→    \"\"\"\n   247→)\n   248→\n   249→\n   250→def sgd(\n   251→    params: list[Tensor],\n   252→    d_p_list: list[Tensor],\n   253→    momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],\n   254→    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n   255→    # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n   256→    has_sparse_grad: bool = False,\n   257→    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n   258→    fused: Optional[bool] = None,\n   259→    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n   260→    found_inf: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n   261→    *,\n   262→    weight_decay: float,\n   263→    momentum: float,\n   264→    lr: float,\n   265→    dampening: float,\n   266→    nesterov: bool,\n   267→    maximize: bool,\n   268→):\n   269→    r\"\"\"Functional API that performs SGD algorithm computation.\n   270→\n   271→    See :class:`~torch.optim.SGD` for details.\n   272→    \"\"\"\n   273→    # Respect when the user inputs False/True for foreach or fused. We only want to change\n   274→    # the default when neither have been user-specified. Note that we default to foreach\n   275→    # and pass False to use_fused. This is not a mistake--we want to give the fused impl\n   276→    # bake-in time before making it the default, even if it is typically faster.\n   277→    if foreach is None and fused is None:\n   278→        # why must we be explicit about an if statement for torch.jit.is_scripting here?\n   279→        # because JIT can't handle Optionals nor fancy conditionals when scripting\n   280→        else:\n   285→            foreach = False\n   286→            fused = False\n   287→    if foreach is None:\n   288→        foreach = False\n   289→    if fused is None:\n   290→        fused = torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   295→        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with fused optimizers\")\n   296→\n   297→    if foreach and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   298→ 311→        dampening=dampening,\n   312→        nesterov=nesterov,\n   313→        has_sparse_grad=has_sparse_grad,\n   314→        maximize=maximize,\n   315→        grad_scale=grad_scale,\n   316→        found_inf=found_inf,\n   317→    )\n   318→\n   319→\n   320→def _single_tensor_sgd(\n   321→    params: list[Tensor],\n   322→    grads: list[Tensor],\n   323→    momentum_buffer_list:   336→\n   337→    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   338→        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   339→\n   340→    for i, param in enumerate(params):\n   341→        grad = grads[i] if not maximize else -grads[i]\n   342→\n   343→        if weight_decay != 0:\n   344→            # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules\n   345→            if isinstance(weight_decay, Tensor):\n   346→                if weight_decay.requires_grad:\n   347→                    # usually this is the differentiable path, which is why the param.clone() is needed\n   348→                    grad = grad.addcmul_(param.clone(), weight_decay)\n   349→                else:\n   350→                    grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n   351→            else:\n   352→                grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n   353→\n   354→        if momentum != 0:\n   355→            buf = momentum_buffer_list[i]\n   356→\n   357→            if buf is None:\n   358→                buf = grad.detach().clone()\n   359→                momentum_buffer_list[i] = buf\n   360→            else:\n   361→                buf.mul_(momentum).add_(grad, alpha=1 - dampening)\n   362→\n   363→            if nesterov:\n   364→                grad = grad.add(buf, alpha=momentum)\n   365→            else:\n   366→                grad = buf\n   367→\n   368→        # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules\n   369→        if isinstance(lr, Tensor):\n   370→            if lr.requires_grad:\n   371→                param.addcmul_(grad, lr, value=-1)\n   372→            else:\n   373→                param.add_(grad, alpha=-lr)\n   374→        else:\n   375→            param.add_(grad, alpha=-lr)\n   376→\n   377→\n   378→def _multi_tensor_sgd(\n   379→    params: list[Tensor],\n   380→    grads: list[Tensor],\n   381→    momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],\n   382→    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n   383→    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n   384→    *,\n   385→ 392→):\n   393→    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n   394→\n   395→    if len(params) == 0:\n   396→        return\n   397→\n   398→    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   399→\n   400→ has_sparse_grad and any(\n   413→            grad.is_sparse for grad in device_grads\n   414→        )\n   415→\n   416→        if maximize:\n   417→            device_grads = torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   418→\n   419→        if weight_decay != 0:\n   420→            # Reuse the intermediate memory (device_grads) already allocated for maximize\n   421→            if maximize:\n   422→                torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n   423→            else:\n   424→                device_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   425→                    device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay\n   426→                )\n   427→\n   428→        if momentum != 0:\n    bufs.append(cast(Tensor, device_momentum_buffer_list[i]))\n   438→\n   439→            if all_states_with_momentum_buffer:\n   440→                torch._foreach_mul_(bufs, momentum)\n   441→                torch._foreach_add_(bufs, device_grads, alpha=1 - dampening)\n   442→            else:\n   443→                bufs = []\n   444→                for i in    buf = cast(Tensor, device_momentum_buffer_list[i])\n   451→                        buf.mul_(momentum).add_(device_grads[i], alpha=1 - dampening)\n   452→\n   453→                    bufs.append(buf)\n   454→\n   455→            if nesterov:\n   456→                torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, bufs, alpha=momentum)\n   457→            else:\n   458→                device_grads = bufs\n   459→\n   460→        if not device_has_sparse_grad:\n   461→            # handle internal item() call if lr is a tensor\n   462→            if isinstance(lr, torch.Tensor) and  &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   206→            &\\textbf{input}      : \\alpha \\text{ (alpha)}, \\: \\gamma \\text{ (lr)},\n   207→                \\: \\theta_0 \\text{ (params)}, \\: f(\\theta) \\text{ (objective)}                   \\\\\n   208→            &\\hspace{13mm}   \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)},\\: \\mu \\text{ (momentum)},\n   209→                \\: centered, \\: \\epsilon \\text{ (epsilon)}                                       \\\\\n   210→            &\\textbf{initialize} : v_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ (square average)}, \\:\n   211→                \\textbf{b}_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ (buffer)}, \\: g^{ave}_0 \\leftarrow 0     \\\\[-1.ex]\n   212→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   213→            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n   214→            &\\hspace{5mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})           \\\\\n   215→            &\\hspace{5mm}if \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                                    \\\\\n   216→            &\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda  \\theta_{t-1}                            \\\\\n   217→            &\\hspace{5mm}v_t           \\leftarrow   \\alpha v_{t-1} + (1 - \\alpha) g^2_t\n   218→                \\hspace{8mm}                                                                     \\\\\n   219→            &\\hspace{5mm} \\tilde{v_t} \\leftarrow v_t                                             \\\\\n   220→            &\\hspace{5mm}if \\: centered                                                          \\\\\n   221→            &\\hspace{10mm} g^{ave}_t \\leftarrow g^{ave}_{t-1} \\alpha + (1-\\alpha) g_t            \\\\\n   222→            &\\hspace{10mm} \\tilde{v_t} \\leftarrow \\tilde{v_t} -  \\big(g^{ave}_{t} \\big)^2        \\\\\n   223→            &\\hspace{5mm}if \\: \\mu > 0                                                           \\\\\n   224→            &\\hspace{10mm} \\textbf{b}_t\\leftarrow \\mu \\textbf{b}_{t-1} +\n   225→                g_t/ \\big(\\sqrt{\\tilde{v_t}} +  \\epsilon \\big)                                   \\\\\n   226→            &\\hspace{10mm} \\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1} - \\gamma \\textbf{b}_t                \\\\\n   227→            &\\hspace{5mm} else                                                                   \\\\\n   228→            &\\hspace{10mm}\\theta_t      \\leftarrow   \\theta_{t-1} -\n   229→                \\gamma  g_t/ \\big(\\sqrt{\\tilde{v_t}} + \\epsilon \\big)  \\hspace{3mm}              \\\\\n   230→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   231→            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n   232→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   233→       \\end{aligned}\n   234→\n   235→    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to\n   236→    `lecture notes <https://www.cs.toronto.edu/~tijmen/csc321/slides/lecture_slides_lec6.pdf>`_ by G. Hinton.\n   237→    and centered version `Generating Sequences\n   238→    With Recurrent Neural Networks <https://arxiv.org/pdf/1308.0850v5.pdf>`_.\n   239→    The implementation here takes the square root of the gradient average before\n   240→    adding epsilon (note that TensorFlow interchanges these two operations). The effective\n   241→    learning rate is thus :math:`\\gamma/(\\sqrt{v} + \\epsilon)` where :math:`\\gamma`\n   242→    is the scheduled learning rate and :math:`v` is the weighted moving average\n   243→    of the squared gradient.\n   244→    \"\"\"\n   245→    + rf\"\"\"\n   246→    Args:\n   247→        {_params_doc}\n   248→        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-2)\n   249→        alpha (float, optional): smoothing constant (default: 0.99)\n   250→        eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve\n   251→            numerical stability (default: 1e-8)\n   252→        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n   253→        momentum (float, optional): momentum factor (default: 0)\n   254→        centered (bool, optional) : if ``True``, compute the centered RMSProp,\n   255→            the gradient is normalized by an estimation of its variance\n   256→        {_capturable_doc}\n   257→        {_foreach_doc}\n   258→        {_maximize_doc}\n   259→        {_differentiable_doc}\n   260→\n   261→    \"\"\"\n   262→)\n   263→\n   264→\n   265→def _single_tensor_rmsprop(\n   266→    params: list[Tensor],\n   272→    *,\n   273→    lr: float,\n   274→    alpha: float,\n   275→    eps: float,\n   276→    weight_decay: float,\n   277→    momentum: float,\n   278→    centered: bool,\n   279→    maximize: bool,\n   280→    differentiable: bool,\n   281→    capturable: bool,\n   282→    has_complex: bool,\n   283→):\n   284→    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   285→        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   286→\n   287→    for i, param in enumerate(params):\n   288→        step = state_steps[i]\n   289→\n   290→        # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see          assert (\n   294→                param.device.type == step.device.type\n   295→                and param.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n   296→            ), (\n   297→                f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n   298→            )\n   299→\n   300→        grad = grads[i]\n   301→        grad = grad if not maximize else -grad\n   302→        square_avg = square_avgs[i]\n   303→\n   304→        step += 1\n   305→\n   306→        if weight_decay != 0:\n   307→            grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n   308→\n   309→        is_complex_param = torch.is_complex(param)\n   310→        if is_complex_param:\n   311→            param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n   312→            grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n   313→            square_avg = torch.view_as_real(square_avg)\n   314→\n   315→        square_avg.mul_(alpha).addcmul_(grad, grad, value=1 - alpha)\n   316→\n   317→        if centered:\n   318→            grad_avg = grad_avgs[i]\n   319→            if is_complex_param:\n   320→                grad_avg = torch.view_as_real(grad_avg)\n   321→ 326→        if differentiable:\n   327→            avg = avg.add(eps)\n   328→        else:\n   329→            avg = avg.add_(eps)\n   330→\n   331→        if momentum > 0:\n   332→            buf = momentum_buffer_list[i]\n   333→            if is_complex_param:\n   334→                buf = torch.view_as_real(buf)\n   335→            buf.mul_(momentum).addcdiv_(grad, avg)\n   336→            param.add_(buf, alpha=-lr)\n   337→        else:\n   338→            param.addcdiv_(grad, avg, value=-lr)\n   339→\n   340→\n   341→def _multi_tensor_rmsprop(\n   342→    params: list[Tensor],\n torch._foreach_neg(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   410→\n   411→        # Update steps\n   412→        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n   413→        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n   414→        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n   415→        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and grouped_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n   416→            torch._foreach_add_(\n   417→                grouped_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n   418→            )\n   419→        else:\n   420→            torch._foreach_add_(grouped_state_steps, 1)\n   421→\n   422→        if weight_decay != 0:\n   423→            #           device_state_sums,\n   437→                device_state_steps,\n   438→                lr=lr,\n   439→                weight_decay=weight_decay,\n   440→                lr_decay=lr_decay,\n Handle complex parameters\n   452→        if has_complex:\n   453→            _view_as_real(device_params, device_grads, device_state_sums)\n   454→\n   455→        if maximize:\n   456→            device_grads = torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   457→\n   458→        # Update steps\n   459→        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n   460→        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n   461→        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n   462→        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and device_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n   463→            torch._foreach_add_(\n   464→                device_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n   465→            )\n   466→        else:\n   467→            torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n   468→\n   469→        if weight_decay != 0:\n   470→            # Reuse the intermediate memory (device_grads) already allocated for maximize\n   471→            if maximize:\n   472→                torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n   473→            else:\n   474→                device_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   475→                    device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay\n   476→                )\n   477→\n   478→        minus_clr = [\n   479→            -lr / (1 + (_get_value(step) - 1) * lr_decay) for step in device_state_steps\n   480→        ]\n   481→\n   482→        torch._foreach_addcmul_(device_state_sums, device_grads, device_grads, value=1)\n   483→\n   484→        std = torch._foreach_sqrt(device_state_sums)\n   485→        torch._foreach_add_(std, eps)\n   486→\n   487→        if weight_decay != 0 or maximize:\n   488→            # Again, reuse the intermediate memory (device_grads) already allocated\n   489→            torch._foreach_mul_(device_grads, minus_clr)\n   490→            numerator = device_grads\n   491→        else:\n   492→            numerator = torch._foreach_mul(device_grads, minus_clr)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   493→\n   494→        torch._foreach_addcdiv_(device_params, numerator, std)\n   495→\n   496→\n   497→def _fused_adagrad(\n   498→    params: list[Tensor],\n   499→    grads: list[Tensor],\n   500→    state_sums: list[Tensor],\n   501→    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   502→    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n   503→    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n   504→    *,\n   505→    lr: float,\n   506→    weight_decay: float,\n   507→    lr_decay: float,\n   508→    eps: float,\n   509→    has_sparse_grad: bool,\n   510→    maximize: bool,\n   511→    differentiable: bool,\n   512→    has_complex: bool,\n   513→) -> None:\n   514→    if not params:\n   515→        return\n   516→    if has_sparse_grad or has_complex:\n   517→        raise RuntimeError(\"`fused` does not support sparse grad or complex param\")\n   518→\n   519→    if differentiable:\n   520→        raise RuntimeError(\n   521→            \"adagrad with fused=True does not support differentiable=True\"\n   522→        )\n   523→\n   524→    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   525→\n   526→    grad_scale_dict = (\n   527→        {grad_scale.device: grad_scale} if grad_scale is not None else None\n   528→    )\n   529→    found_inf_dict = {found_inf.device: found_inf} if found_inf is not None else None\n   530→\n   531→    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n   532→        [params, grads, state_sums, state_steps]  # type: ignore[index]\n   552→            device_grad_scale = grad_scale_dict[device]  # type: ignore[index]\n   553→        if found_inf is not None and found_inf_dict is not None:\n   554→            if found_inf not in found_inf_dict:\n   555→                found_inf_dict[device] = found_inf.to(device, non_blocking=True)  # type: ignore[index]\n   556→            device_found_inf = found_inf_dict[device]  # type: ignore[index]\n   557→        torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n   558→        torch._fused_adagrad_(\n   559→            device_params,\n   560→            device_grads,\n   561→            device_state_sums,\n   562→            device_state_steps,\n   563→            lr=lr,\n   564→            lr_decay=lr_decay,\n   565→            weight_decay=weight_decay,\n   566→            eps=eps,\n   567→            maximize=maximize,\n   568→            grad_scale=device_grad_scale,\n   569→            found_inf=device_found_inf,\n   570→        )\n   571→        if device_found_inf is not None:\n   572→            torch._foreach_sub_(\n   573→                device_state_steps, [device_found_inf] * len(device_state_steps)\n   574→            )\n   575→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. algorithm.\n   276→\n   277→    .. math::\n   278→       \\begin{aligned}\n   279→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   280→            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma \\text{ (lr)}, \\beta_1, \\beta_2\n   281→                \\text{ (betas)},\\theta_0 \\text{ (params)},f(\\theta) \\text{ (objective)}          \\\\\n   282→            &\\hspace{13mm}      \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)},  \\: \\textit{amsgrad},\n   283→                \\:\\textit{maximize},  \\: \\epsilon \\text{ (epsilon)}                              \\\\\n   284→            &\\textbf{initialize} :  m_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ ( first moment)},\n   285→                v_0\\leftarrow 0 \\text{ (second moment)},\\: v_0^{max}\\leftarrow 0          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   286→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   287→            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n   288→\n   289→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{maximize}:                                       \\\\\n   290→            &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   -\\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})         \\\\\n   291→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{else}                                                           \\\\\n   292→            &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})          \\\\\n   293→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                           \\\\\n   294→            &\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda  \\theta_{t-1}                            \\\\\n   295→            &\\hspace{5mm}m_t           \\leftarrow   \\beta_1 m_{t-1} + (1 - \\beta_1) g_t          \\\\\n   296→            &\\hspace{5mm}v_t           \\leftarrow   \\beta_2 v_{t-1} + (1-\\beta_2) g^2_t          \\\\\n   297→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\widehat{m_t} \\leftarrow   m_t/\\big(1-\\beta_1^t \\big)                   \\\\\n   298→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: amsgrad                                                  \\\\\n   299→            &\\hspace{10mm} v_t^{max} \\leftarrow \\mathrm{max}(v_{t-1}^{max},v_t)                  \\\\\n   300→            &\\hspace{10mm}\\widehat{v_t} \\leftarrow v_t^{max}/\\big(1-\\beta_2^t \\big)              \\\\\n   301→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{else}                                                           \\\\\n   302→            &\\hspace{10mm}\\widehat{v_t} \\leftarrow   v_t/\\big(1-\\beta_2^t \\big)                  \\\\\n   303→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1} - \\gamma \\widehat{m_t}/\n   304→                \\big(\\sqrt{\\widehat{v_t}} + \\epsilon \\big)                                       \\\\\n   305→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   306→            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n   307→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   308→       \\end{aligned}\n   309→\n   310→    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to `Adam: A Method for Stochastic Optimization`_.\n   311→    \"\"\"\n   312→    + rf\"\"\"\n   313→    Args:\n   314→        {_params_doc}\n   315→        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-3). A tensor LR\n   316→            is not yet supported for all our implementations. Please use a float\n   317→            LR if you are not also specifying fused=True or capturable=True.\n   318→        betas (Tuple[float, float], optional): coefficients used for computing\n   319→            running averages of gradient and its square (default: (0.9, 0.999))\n   320→        eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve\n   321→            numerical stability (default: 1e-8)\n   322→        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n   323→        decoupled_weight_decay (bool, optional): if True, this optimizer is\n   324→            equivalent to AdamW and the algorithm will not accumulate weight\n   325→            decay in the momentum nor variance. (default: False)\n   326→        amsgrad (bool, optional): whether to use the AMSGrad variant of this\n   327→            algorithm from the paper `On the Convergence of Adam and Beyond`_\n   328→            (default: False)\n   329→        {_foreach_doc}\n   330→        {_maximize_doc}\n   331→        {_capturable_doc}\n   332→        {_differentiable_doc}\n   333→        {_fused_doc}\n   334→    .. 344→\n   345→def _single_tensor_adam(\n   346→    params: list[Tensor],\n   347→    grads: list[Tensor],\n   348→    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   349→    exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n   350→
