found_inf is None\n   368→\n   369→    if torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   370→        # this assert is due to JIT being dumb and not realizing that the ops below\n   371→        # have overloads to handle both float and Tensor lrs, so we just assert it's\n   372→        # a float since most people using JIT are using floats\n   373→        assert isinstance(lr, float)\n   374→        assert isinstance(beta1, float)\n   375→        assert isinstance(beta2, float)\n   376→    else:\n   377→        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   378→        # TODO: Support nonzero-dim Tensor betas, see #147921\n   379→\n   380→    # We only shuffle around the beta when it is a Tensor, otherwise, we prefer\n   381→    # treating it as a scalar.\n   382→    # Note: ensure type declaration is under conditional check for isinstance\n   383→    # or else torchscript will get cranky about the DeviceDict type.\n   384→    if isinstance(beta1, Tensor):\n   385→        beta1_dict: Optional[DeviceDtypeDict] = {(beta1.device, beta1.dtype): beta1}\n   386→    else:\n   387→        beta1_dict = None\n   388→\n   389→    for i, param in enumerate(params):\n   390→        grad = grads[i] if not maximize else -grads[i]\n   391→        exp_avg = exp_avgs[i]\n   392→        exp_avg_sq = exp_avg_sqs[i]\n   393→        step_t = state_steps[i]\n   394→\n   395→        # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n   396→        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n   397→            capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices()\n   398→            assert (\n   399→                param.device.type == step_t.device.type\n   400→                and param.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n   401→            ), (\n   402→                f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n   403→            )\n   404→\n   405→        # update step\n   406→        step_t += 1\n   407→\n   408→        if weight_decay != 0:\n   409→            if decoupled_weight_decay:\n   410→                # Perform stepweight decay\n   411→                param.mul_(1 - lr * weight_decay)\n   412→            else:\n   413→                # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules\n   414→                if differentiable and isinstance(weight_decay, Tensor):\n   415→                    if weight_decay.requires_grad:\n   416→                        grad = grad.addcmul_(param.clone(), weight_decay)\n   417→                    else:\n   418→                        grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n   419→                else:\n   420→                    grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n   421→\n   422→        if torch.is_complex(param):\n   423→            grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n   424→            exp_avg = torch.view_as_real(exp_avg)\n   425→            exp_avg_sq = torch.view_as_real(exp_avg_sq)\n   426→            if amsgrad:\n   427→                max_exp_avg_sqs[i] = torch.view_as_real(max_exp_avg_sqs[i])\n   428→            param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n   429→\n   430→        device = param.device\n   431→\n   432→        if beta1_dict is not None:\n   433→            dtype = param.dtype  # type: ignore[union-attr]\n   434→\n   435→            # cast to workaround https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/140601\n   436→            key = (device, dtype)\n   437→            if key not in beta1_dict:\n   438→                beta1_dict[key] = beta1.to(  # type: ignore[union-attr]\n   439→                    device=device, dtype=dtype, non_blocking=True\n   440→                )\n   441→\n   442→            device_beta1: Union[float, Tensor] = beta1_dict[key]\n   443→        else:\n   444→            device_beta1 = beta1\n   445→\n   446→        # Decay the first and second moment running average coefficient\n   447→        exp_avg.lerp_(grad, 1 - device_beta1)\n   448→\n   449→        # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules\n   450→        if differentiable and isinstance(beta2, Tensor):\n   451→            if beta2.requires_grad:\n   452→                # Using lerp to only use 2 operations bc addcmul's value cannot be a tensor\n   453→                # Showing equivalence of differentiable path and nondifferentiable path\n   454→                # expavg * b2 + grad^2 * (1-b2)\n   455→                #           add expavg * (1-b2) - expavg * (1-b2) = 0\n   456→                # expavg * b2 + expavg * (1-b2) - expavg * (1-b2) + grad^2 * (1-b2)\n   457→                # expavg - expavg * (1-b2) + grad^2 * (1-b2)\n   458→                # expavg + (grad^2 - expavg) * (1-b2)\n   459→                # expavg.lerp(grad^2, 1-beta2)\n   460→                exp_avg_sq.lerp_(torch.square(grad), weight=1 - beta2)\n   461→            else:\n   462→                exp_avg_sq.mul_(beta2).addcmul_(\n   463→                    grad, grad, value=cast(float, 1 - beta2)\n   464→                )\n   465→        else:\n   466→            exp_avg_sq.mul_(beta2).addcmul_(grad, grad, value=1 - beta2)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n   467→\n   468→        if capturable or differentiable:\n   469→            step = step_t\n   470→\n   471→            # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules\n   472→            if differentiable and isinstance(beta1, Tensor):\n   473→                if beta1.requires_grad:\n   474→                    bias_correction1 = 1 - beta1 ** step.clone()\n   475→                else:\n   476→                    bias_correction1 = 1 - beta1**step\n   477→            else:\n   478→                bias_correction1 = 1 - beta1**step\n   479→\n   480→            # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules\n   481→            if differentiable and isinstance(beta2, Tensor):\n   482→                if beta2.requires_grad:\n   483→                    bias_correction2 = 1 - beta2 ** step.clone()\n   484→                else:\n   485→                    bias_correction2 = 1 - beta2**step\n   486→            else:\n   487→                bias_correction2 = 1 - beta2**step\n   488→\n   489→            step_size = lr / bias_correction1\n   490→            step_size_neg = step_size.neg()\n   491→\n   492→            bias_correction2_sqrt = bias_correction2.sqrt()\n   493→\n   494→            if amsgrad:\n   495→                # Maintains the maximum of all 2nd moment running avg. till now\n   496→                if differentiable:\n   497→                    max_exp_avg_sq = max_exp_avg_sqs[i].clone()\n   498→                else:\n   499→                    max_exp_avg_sq = max_exp_avg_sqs[i]\n   500→\n   501→                max_exp_avg_sqs[i].copy_(torch.maximum(max_exp_avg_sq, exp_avg_sq))\n   502→\n   503→                # Uses the max. for normalizing running avg. of gradient\n   504→                # Folds in (admittedly ugly) 1-elem step_size math here to avoid extra param-set-sized read+write\n   505→                # (can't fold it into addcdiv_ below because addcdiv_ requires value is a Number, not a Tensor)\n   506→                denom = (\n   507→                    max_exp_avg_sqs[i].sqrt() / (bias_correction2_sqrt * step_size_neg)\n   508→                ).add_(eps / step_size_neg)\n   509→            else:\n   510→                denom = (\n   511→                    exp_avg_sq.sqrt() / (bias_correction2_sqrt * step_size_neg)\n   512→                ).add_(eps / step_size_neg)\n   513→\n   514→            if differentiable:\n   515→                param.addcdiv_(exp_avg.clone(), denom)\n   516→            else:\n   517→                param.addcdiv_(exp_avg, denom)\n   518→        else:\n   519→            step = _get_value(step_t)\n   520→\n   521→            bias_correction1 = 1 - beta1**step\n   522→            bias_correction2 = 1 - beta2**step\n   523→\n   524→            step_size = lr / bias_correction1\n   525→\n   526→            bias_correction2_sqrt = bias_correction2**0.5\n   527→\n   528→            if amsgrad:\n   529→                # Maintains the maximum of all 2nd moment running avg. till now\n   530→                torch.maximum(max_exp_avg_sqs[i], exp_avg_sq, out=max_exp_avg_sqs[i])\n   531→\n   532→                # Use the max. for normalizing running avg. of gradient\n   533→                denom = (max_exp_avg_sqs[i].sqrt() / bias_correction2_sqrt).add_(eps)\n   534→            else:\n   535→                denom = (exp_avg_sq.sqrt() / bias_correction2_sqrt).add_(eps)\n   536→\n   537→            param.addcdiv_(exp_avg, denom, value=-step_size)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n   538→\n   539→        # Lastly, switch back to complex view\n   540→        if amsgrad and torch.is_complex(params[i]):\n   541→            max_exp_avg_sqs[i] = torch.view_as_complex(max_exp_avg_sqs[i])\n   542→\n   543→\n   544→def _multi_tensor_adam(\n   545→    params: list[Tensor],\n   546→    grads: list[Tensor],\n   547→    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   548→    exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n   549→    max_exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n   550→    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   551→    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n   552→    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n   553→    *,\n   554→    amsgrad: bool,\n   555→    has_complex: bool,\n   556→    beta1: Union[float, Tensor],\n   557→    beta2: Union[float, Tensor],\n   558→    lr: Union[float, Tensor],\n   559→    weight_decay: float,\n   560→    eps: float,\n   561→    maximize: bool,\n   562→    capturable: bool,\n   563→    differentiable: bool,\n   564→    decoupled_weight_decay: bool,\n   565→):\n   566→    if len(params) == 0:\n   567→        return\n   568→\n   569→    if isinstance(lr, Tensor):\n   570→        if not capturable:\n   571→            raise RuntimeError(\n   572→                \"lr as a Tensor is not supported for capturable=False and foreach=True\"\n   573→            )\n   574→        if lr.numel() != 1:\n   575→            raise ValueError(\"Tensor lr must be 1-element\")\n   576→\n   577→    if isinstance(beta1, Tensor):\n   578→        if not capturable:\n   579→            raise ValueError(\n   580→                \"beta1 as a Tensor is not supported for capturable=False and foreach=True\"\n   581→            )\n   582→        if beta1.numel() != 1:\n   583→            raise ValueError(\"Tensor beta1 must be 1-element\")\n   584→\n   585→    if isinstance(beta2, Tensor):\n   586→        if not capturable:\n   587→            raise ValueError(\n   588→                \"beta2 as a Tensor is not supported for capturable=False and foreach=True\"\n   589→            )\n   590→        if beta2.numel() != 1:\n   591→            raise ValueError(\"Tensor beta2 must be 1-element\")\n   592→\n   593→    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n   594→    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n   595→        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(\n   596→            supports_xla=False\n   597→        )\n   598→        assert all(\n   599→            p.device.type == step.device.type\n   600→            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n   601→            for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)\n   602→        ), (\n   603→            f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n   604→        )\n   605→\n   606→    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n   607→\n   608→    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n   609→\n   610→    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   611→    # TODO: Support nonzero-dim Tensor betas, see #147921\n   612→\n   613→    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n   614→        [params, grads, exp_avgs, exp_avg_sqs, max_exp_avg_sqs, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n   615→    )\n   616→\n   617→    # We only shuffle around the beta when it is a Tensor and on CUDA, otherwise, we prefer\n   618→    # treating it as a scalar.\n   619→    beta1_dict: Optional[DeviceDict] = (  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n   620→        {beta1.device: beta1}\n   621→        if isinstance(beta1, Tensor) and str(beta1.device) != \"cpu\"\n   622→        else None\n   623→    )\n   624→\n   625→    for (\n   626→        device_params_,\n   627→        device_grads_,\n   628→        device_exp_avgs_,\n   629→        device_exp_avg_sqs_,\n   630→        device_max_exp_avg_sqs_,\n   631→        device_state_steps_,\n   632→    ), _ in grouped_tensors.values():\n   633→        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n   634→        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n   635→        device_exp_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], device_exp_avgs_)\n   636→        device_exp_avg_sqs = cast(list[Tensor], device_exp_avg_sqs_)\n   637→        device_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_steps_)\n   638→\n   639→        device = device_params[0].device\n   640→        if beta1_dict is not None and device not in beta1_dict:\n   641→            beta1_dict[device] = beta1.to(device=device, non_blocking=True)  # type: ignore[union-attr, attr-defined]\n   642→\n   643→        device_beta1 = beta1_dict[device] if beta1_dict else beta1\n   644→\n   645→        # Handle complex parameters\n   646→        if has_complex:\n   647→            if amsgrad:\n   648→                device_max_exp_avg_sqs = cast(list[Tensor], device_max_exp_avg_sqs_)\n   649→                _view_as_real(\n   650→                    device_params,\n   651→                    device_grads,\n   652→                    device_exp_avgs,\n   653→                    device_exp_avg_sqs,\n   654→                    device_max_exp_avg_sqs,\n   655→                )\n   656→            else:\n   657→                _view_as_real(\n   658→                    device_params, device_grads, device_exp_avgs, device_exp_avg_sqs\n   659→                )\n   660→\n   661→        if maximize:\n   662→            device_grads = torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   663→\n   664→        # Update steps\n   665→        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n   666→        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n   667→        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n   668→        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and device_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n   669→            torch._foreach_add_(\n   670→                device_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n   671→            )\n   672→        else:\n   673→            torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n   674→\n   675→        if weight_decay != 0:\n   676→            if decoupled_weight_decay:\n   677→                # Perform stepweight decay\n   678→                torch._foreach_mul_(device_params, 1 - lr * weight_decay)\n   679→            else:\n   680→                # Reuse the intermediate memory (device_grads) already allocated for maximize\n   681→                if maximize:\n   682→                    torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n   683→                else:\n   684→                    device_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   685→                        device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay\n   686→                    )\n   687→\n   688→        # Decay the first and second moment running average coefficient\n   689→        # Use device beta1 if beta1 is a tensor to ensure all\n   690→        # tensors are on the same device\n   691→        torch._foreach_lerp_(\n   692→            device_exp_avgs, device_grads, cast(float, 1 - device_beta1)\n   693→        )\n   694→\n   695→        torch._foreach_mul_(device_exp_avg_sqs, beta2)\n   696→\n   697→        # Due to the strictness of the _foreach_addcmul API, we can't have a single\n   698→        # tensor scalar as the scalar arg (only python number is supported there)\n   699→        # as a result, separate out the value mul\n   700→        # Filed https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/139795\n   701→        if isinstance(beta2, torch.Tensor):\n   702→            scaled_device_grads = torch._foreach_mul(device_grads, 1 - beta2)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   703→            value = 1.0\n   704→        else:\n   705→            scaled_device_grads = device_grads  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   706→            value = 1 - beta2\n   707→\n   708→        torch._foreach_addcmul_(\n   709→            device_exp_avg_sqs, scaled_device_grads, device_grads, value\n   710→        )\n   711→\n   712→        # Delete the local intermediate(s) since they won't be used anymore to save on peak memory\n   713→        del device_grads\n   714→        del scaled_device_grads\n   715→\n   716→        bias_correction1: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   717→        bias_correction2: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   718→        bias_correction2_sqrt: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   719→\n   720→        if capturable:\n   721→            bias_correction1 = torch._foreach_pow(beta1, device_state_steps)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n   722→            bias_correction2 = torch._foreach_pow(beta2, device_state_steps)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n   723→            # foreach_sub doesn't allow a scalar as the first arg\n   724→            torch._foreach_sub_(bias_correction1, 1)\n   725→            torch._foreach_sub_(bias_correction2, 1)\n   726→            # we do not negate bias_correction1 as it'll need to be negated later anyway\n   727→            torch._foreach_neg_(bias_correction2)\n   728→\n   729→            # foreach_div doesn't allow a scalar as the first arg\n   730→            torch._foreach_div_(bias_correction1, lr)\n   731→            torch._foreach_reciprocal_(bias_correction1)\n   732→\n   733→            torch._foreach_sqrt_(bias_correction2)\n   734→\n   735→            # Re-assign for clarity as we maintain minimal intermediates: we'll have\n   736→            # step_size = - lr / (1 - beta1 ^ t) where t = num_steps\n   737→            # bias_correction2_sqrt = sqrt(1 - beta2 ^ t)\n   738→            step_size = bias_correction1\n   739→            bias_correction2_sqrt = bias_correction2\n   740→\n   741→            if amsgrad:\n   742→                device_max_exp_avg_sqs = cast(list[Tensor], device_max_exp_avg_sqs_)\n   743→                # Maintains the maximum of all 2nd moment running avg. till now\n   744→                torch._foreach_maximum_(device_max_exp_avg_sqs, device_exp_avg_sqs)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   745→\n   746→                # Set intermediate to the max. for normalizing running avg. of gradient when amsgrad\n   747→                exp_avg_sq_sqrt = torch._foreach_sqrt(device_max_exp_avg_sqs)\n   748→            else:\n   749→                exp_avg_sq_sqrt = torch._foreach_sqrt(device_exp_avg_sqs)\n   750→\n   751→            torch._foreach_div_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, bias_correction2_sqrt)\n   752→            torch._foreach_add_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, eps)\n   753→            torch._foreach_div_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, step_size)\n   754→\n   755→            # at this point, exp_avg_sq_sqrt = - (1 - beta^t) * [sqrt(exp_avg_sq / (1 - beta2^t)) + eps] / lr\n   756→            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(device_params, device_exp_avgs, exp_avg_sq_sqrt)\n   757→        else:\n   758→            bias_correction1 = [\n   759→                1 - beta1 ** _get_value(step) for step in device_state_steps\n   760→            ]\n   761→            bias_correction2 = [\n   762→                1 - beta2 ** _get_value(step) for step in device_state_steps\n   763→            ]\n   764→\n   765→            step_size = _stack_if_compiling([(lr / bc) * -1 for bc in bias_correction1])\n   766→\n   767→            bias_correction2_sqrt = [bc**0.5 for bc in bias_correction2]  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n   768→\n   769→            if amsgrad:\n   770→                device_max_exp_avg_sqs = cast(list[Tensor], device_max_exp_avg_sqs_)\n   771→                # Maintains the maximum of all 2nd moment running avg. till now\n   772→                torch._foreach_maximum_(device_max_exp_avg_sqs, device_exp_avg_sqs)\n   773→\n   774→                # Use the max. for normalizing running avg. of gradient\n   775→                exp_avg_sq_sqrt = torch._foreach_sqrt(device_max_exp_avg_sqs)\n   776→            else:\n   777→                exp_avg_sq_sqrt = torch._foreach_sqrt(device_exp_avg_sqs)\n   778→\n   779→            torch._foreach_div_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, bias_correction2_sqrt)\n   780→            torch._foreach_add_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, eps)\n   781→            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(\n   782→                device_params,\n   783→                device_exp_avgs,\n   784→                exp_avg_sq_sqrt,\n   785→                step_size,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n   786→            )\n   787→\n   788→\n   789→def _fused_adam(\n   790→    params: list[Tensor],\n   791→    grads: list[Tensor],\n   792→    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   793→    exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n   794→    max_exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n   795→    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   796→    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n   797→    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n   798→    *,\n   799→    amsgrad: bool,\n   800→    has_complex: bool,  # Needed for consistency.\n   801→    beta1: float,\n   802→    beta2: float,\n   803→    lr: Union[float, Tensor],\n   804→    weight_decay: float,\n   805→    eps: float,\n   806→    maximize: bool,\n   807→    capturable: bool,  # Needed for consistency.\n   808→    differentiable: bool,\n   809→    decoupled_weight_decay: bool,\n   810→) -> None:\n   811→    if not params:\n   812→        return\n   813→    if differentiable:\n   814→        raise RuntimeError(\"Adam with fused=True does not support differentiable=True\")\n   815→\n   816→    grad_scale_dict: DeviceDict = (\n   817→        {grad_scale.device: grad_scale} if grad_scale is not None else {}\n   818→    )\n   819→    found_inf_dict: DeviceDict = (\n   820→        {found_inf.device: found_inf} if found_inf is not None else {}\n   821→    )\n   822→\n   823→    # We only shuffle around the lr when it is a Tensor and on CUDA, otherwise, we prefer\n   824→    # treating it as a scalar.\n   825→    lr_dict: Optional[DeviceDict] = (\n   826→        {lr.device: lr} if isinstance(lr, Tensor) and str(lr.device) != \"cpu\" else None\n   827→    )\n   828→    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n   829→        [params, grads, exp_avgs, exp_avg_sqs, max_exp_avg_sqs, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n   830→    )\n   831→    for (device, _), (\n   832→        (\n   833→            device_params_,\n   834→            device_grads_,\n   835→            device_exp_avgs_,\n   836→            device_exp_avg_sqs_,\n   837→            device_max_exp_avg_sqs,\n   838→            device_state_steps_,\n   839→        ),\n   840→        _,\n   841→    ) in grouped_tensors.items():\n   842→        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n   843→        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n   844→        device_exp_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], device_exp_avgs_)\n   845→        device_exp_avg_sqs = cast(list[Tensor], device_exp_avg_sqs_)\n   846→        device_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_steps_)\n   847→\n   848→        device_grad_scale, device_found_inf = None, None\n   849→        if grad_scale is not None:\n   850→            device_grad_scale = grad_scale_dict.setdefault(\n   851→                device, grad_scale.to(device, non_blocking=True)\n   852→            )\n   853→        if found_inf is not None:\n   854→            device_found_inf = found_inf_dict.setdefault(\n   855→                device, found_inf.to(device, non_blocking=True)\n   856→            )\n   857→        if lr_dict is not None and device not in lr_dict:\n   858→            lr_dict[device] = lr.to(device=device, non_blocking=True)  # type: ignore[union-attr]\n   859→            lr = lr_dict[device]\n   860→        torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n   861→        func = torch._fused_adam_ if not decoupled_weight_decay else torch._fused_adamw_\n   862→        func(\n   863→            device_params,\n   864→            device_grads,\n   865→            device_exp_avgs,\n   866→            device_exp_avg_sqs,\n   867→            device_max_exp_avg_sqs,  # SPECIFIC DIFFERENCES IN STEP LOGIC\n\n#### SGD (_multi_tensor_sgd)\n**Momentum handling (lines 428-458)**:\n- Checks if ALL momentum buffers exist to batch update\n- If all exist: use `_foreach_mul_` and `_foreach_add_` for efficiency\n- If any missing: falls back to loop with `.detach().clone()` for new buffers\n- Nesterov modifies gradients: `torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, bufs, alpha=momentum)`\n\n**Sparse gradient handling (lines 460-470)**:\n```\nif not device_has_sparse_grad:\n    torch._foreach_add_(device_params, device_grads, alpha=-lr)\nelse:\n    for i in range(len(device_params)):  # fallback to loop\n        device_params[i].add_(device_grads[i], alpha=-lr)\n```\n\n#### Adam (_multi_tensor_adam)\n**Parameter validation (lines 569-591)**:\n- Validates tensor lr, beta1, beta2 dimensionality (must be 1-element)\n- Checks capturable compatibility with tensor parameters\n\n**Step increment (lines 668-673)**:\n```\nif not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and device_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n    torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0)\nelse:\n    torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n```\nSpecial handling for CPU-resident steps (avoids repeated tensor wrapping).\n\n**Decoupled weight decay (lines 675-686)**:\n```\nif decoupled_weight_decay:\n    torch._foreach_mul_(device_params, 1 - lr * weight_decay)\nelse:\n    # standard weight decay\n```\n\n**Beta handling for tensor betas (lines 619-623, 640-643)**:\n```\nbeta1_dict: Optional[DeviceDict] = (\n    {beta1.device: beta1} if isinstance(beta1, Tensor) and str(beta1.device) != \"cpu\"\n    else None\n)\n```\nOnly creates device dict for CUDA tensors.\n\n**Moment computation with lerp (lines 691-710)**:\n```\ntorch._foreach_lerp_(device_exp_avgs, device_grads, cast(float, 1 - device_beta1))\ntorch._foreach_mul_(device_exp_avg_sqs, beta2)\ntorch._foreach_addcmul_(device_exp_avg_sqs, scaled_device_grads, device_grads, value)\n```\n\n**Bias correction - Two paths (capturable vs non-capturable, lines 720-786)**:\n- **Capturable**: Uses `_foreach_pow`, `_foreach_sub_`, `_foreach_neg_`, etc. on tensors\n- **Non-capturable**: Python list comprehensions with `_get_value()` extraction\n\n**AMSGrad handling (lines 741-749, 769-775)**:\n```\ntorch._foreach_maximum_(device_max_exp_avg_sqs, device_exp_avg_sqs)\nexp_avg_sq_sqrt = torch._foreach_sqrt(device_max_exp_avg_sqs)\n```\n\n#### RMSprop (_multi_tensor_rmsprop)\n**Complex parameter handling (lines 396-406)**:\n```\nif has_complex:\n    state_and_grads = [grouped_grads, grouped_square_avgs]\n    if momentum > 0:\n        state_and_grads.append(grouped_momentum_buffer_list)\n    if centered:\n        state_and_grads.append(grouped_grad_avgs)\n    _view_as_real(grouped_params, *state_and_grads)\n```\nDynamically builds list of tensors to view as real based on optimizer configuration.\n\n**Step increment with CPU optimization (lines 415-420)**:\nSame pattern as Adam for CPU step tensors.\n\n**Centered variance (lines 436-446)**:\n```\nif centered:\n    torch._foreach_lerp_(grouped_grad_avgs, grouped_grads, 1 - alpha)\n    avg = torch._foreach_addcmul(\n        grouped_square_avgs, grouped_grad_avgs, grouped_grad_avgs, value=-1\n    )\n    torch._foreach_sqrt_(avg)\n    torch._foreach_add_(avg, eps)\nelse:\n    avg = torch._foreach_sqrt(grouped_square_avgs)\n    torch._foreach_add_(avg, eps)\n```\n\n**Tensor LR with capturable (lines 456-470)**:\n```\nif capturable and isinstance(lr, torch.Tensor):\n    momentum_lr = torch._foreach_mul(grouped_momentum_buffer_list, -lr)\n    torch._foreach_add_(grouped_params, momentum_lr)\nelse:\n    torch._foreach_add_(grouped_params, grouped_momentum_buffer_list, alpha=-lr)\n```\nAvoids implicit `.item()` calls when capturing CUDA graphs.\n\n#### Adagrad (_multi_tensor_adagrad)\n**Sparse gradient fallback (lines 428-449)**:\n```\ndevice_has_sparse_grad = has_sparse_grad and any(grad.is_sparse for grad in device_grads)\nif device_has_sparse_grad:\n    _single_tensor_adagrad(device_params, device_grads, ...)\n    continue\n```\nFalls back to single-tensor implementation for sparse gradients.\n\n**Complex parameter handling (line 453)**:\n```\nif has_complex:\n    _view_as_real(device_params, device_grads, device_state_sums)\n```\n\n**Learning rate decay calculation (lines 478-480)**:\n```\nminus_clr = [\n    -lr / (1 + (_get_value(step) - 1) * lr_decay) for step in device_state_steps\n]\n```\nComputes decayed LR per step (per-parameter).\n\n**Gradient reuse pattern (lines 487-492)**:\n```\nif weight_decay != 0 or maximize:\n    torch._foreach_mul_(device_grads, minus_clr)\n    numerator = device_grads\nelse:\n    numerator = torch._foreach_mul(device_grads, minus_clr)\n```\nReuses device_grads if weight_decay or maximize already allocated new memory.\n\n---\n\n### 4. REPEATED CODE PATTERNS ACROSS FUNCTIONS\n\n#### Pattern 1: Cast and Device Extraction\n```python\ndevice_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\ndevice = device_params[0].device  # or similar\n```\nConsistent casting pattern after grouping.\n\n#### Pattern 2: Conditional Foreach Invocation\n```python\nif condition:\n    torch._foreach_operation_(list1, list2, ...)\nelse:\n    # fallback or reuse\n```\nUsed extensively for memory optimization (reusing intermediate allocations).\n\n#### Pattern 3: State Vector Updates\n```python\ntorch._foreach_mul_(state_vector, scale_factor)\ntorch._foreach_add_(state_vector, grad_vector, alpha=factor)\n```\nConsistent pattern for exponential moving averages and state accumulation.\n\n#### Pattern 4: Complex Number Handling\n```python\nif has_complex:\n    _view_as_real(params, grads, state_tensors)\n# ... operations\n# restore complex view implicitly on exit\n```\nHandled uniformly across all optimizers.\n\n#### Pattern 5: Parameter Update\n```python\ntorch._foreach_addcdiv_(device_params, numerator, denominator, value=step_size)\n```\nor\n```python\ntorch._foreach_add_(device_params, update, alpha=-lr)\n```\n\n#### Pattern 6: Epsilon Addition\n```python\ntorch._foreach_add_(avg, eps)  # in-place\n```\n\n#### Pattern 7: CPU Step Optimization (Adam, RMSprop)\n```python\nif not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and step_tensor[0].is_cpu:\n    torch._foreach_add_(state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0)\nelse:\n    torch._foreach_add_(state_steps, 1)\n```\n\n#### Pattern 8: Memory Reuse Decision Tree\n```python\nif maximize:  # if this already creates new memory\n    torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)\nelse:\n    device_grads = torch._foreach_add(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n```\n\n---\n\n### 5. Note that the foreach implementation uses\n   257→            ~ sizeof(params) more peak memory than the for-loop version due to the intermediates\n   258→            being a tensorlist vs just one tensor. If memory is prohibitive, batch fewer\n   259→            parameters through the optimizer at a time or switch this flag to False (default: None)\"\"\"\n   260→\n   261→_fused_doc = r\"\"\"fused (bool, optional): whether the fused implementation is used.\n   262→            Currently, `torch.float64`, `torch.float32`, `torch.float16`, and `torch.bfloat16`\n   263→            are supported. (default: None)\n   264→\n   265→    .. note:: The foreach and fused implementations are typically faster than the for-loop,\n   266→              single-tensor implementation, with fused being theoretically fastest with both\n   267→              vertical and horizontal fusion. As such, if the user has not specified either\n   268→              flag (i.e., when foreach = fused = None), we will attempt defaulting to the foreach\n   269→              implementation when the tensors are all on CUDA. Why not fused? Since the fused\n   270→              implementation is relatively new, we want to give it sufficient bake-in time.\n   271→              To specify fused, pass True for fused. These functions have common preprocessing patterns that can be extracted to reduce duplication.\n\n## Exploration Results\n\n### Common Patterns Found Across _multi_tensor_* Functions\n1. **Empty parameter check**: All return early if `len(params) == 0`\n2. **LR scalar conversion**: `lr = _to_scalar(lr)`\n3. **Tensor grouping**: `Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype([...], with_indices=True)`\n4. **Maximize handling**: `if maximize: device_grads = torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)`\n5. **Weight decay**: Memory-optimized addition with reuse of intermediate tensors\n6. **Assertions**: `assert not differentiable` and `assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None`\n7. **Complex dtype handling**: `_view_as_real(...)` pattern\n8. **CPU step optimization**: Avoid repeated tensor wrapping for CPU-resident state\n\n### Utilities Available for Reuse\nFrom `torch.optim.optimizer`:\n- `_to_scalar()` - Convert tensor LR to scalar\n- `_get_value()` - Extract Python value from tensor\n- `_view_as_real()` - Convert complex tensors for processing\n- `_get_scalar_dtype()`, `_get_capturable_supported_devices()`\n- Type aliases: `DeviceDict`, `DeviceDtypeDict`\n- `Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype()` - Core grouping utility\n\n### Optimizer-Specific Differences\n- **SGD**: Momentum buffer handling, sparse gradient fallback\n- **Adam**: Parameter validation, step increment, decoupled weight decay, AMSGrad logic\n- **RMSprop**: Complex handling, centered variance, CPU step optimization\n- **Adagrad**: Sparse gradient fallback, LR decay per parameter\n- Each has distinct bias correction and state update logic that can't be fully abstracted\n\n## Implementation Strategy\n\n### Approach: Helper Functions + Callback-based Framework\n\nCreate `/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py` with:\n\n1. **Helper functions for common operations**:\n   - `_foreach_apply_maximize(device_grads, maximize)` - Negate gradients if maximizing\n   - `_foreach_apply_weight_decay(device_grads, device_params, weight_decay, maximize)` - Memory-optimized weight decay\n   - `_foreach_group_tensors_and_prepare(params, grads, state_tensors, maximize, weight_decay, **kwargs)` - Groups and preprocesses tensors\n\n2. **Main callback-based orchestration function**:\n   - `_foreach_optimizer_step(params, grads, step_fn, state_tensors, **kwargs)`\n   - Handles grouping, assertions, maximize, weight decay\n   - Calls `step_fn(device_params, device_grads, device_states, indices, **kwargs)` for optimizer-specific logic\n\n3. **Common utility functions**:\n   - `_get_group_device_dtype()` - Extract device/dtype from grouped tensors\n   - `_validate_foreach_hyperparams()` - Common validation for tensor hyperparameters\n\n### Files to Modify\n\n**Primary**:\n- Create: `/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py` with shared logic\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/sgd.py` - Use _foreach_optimizer_step\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/adam.py` - Use _foreach_optimizer_step\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/adamw.py` - Inherits from Adam (automatic)\n\n**Secondary** (to meet ≥3 file requirement + reduce duplication):\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/adagrad.py` - Use shared helpers\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/rmsprop.py` - Use shared helpers\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/radam.py` - Use shared helpers\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/adadelta.py` - Use shared helpers\n\n## Detailed Implementation Plan\n\n### Step 1: Create `torch/optim/_foreach.py`\n\n```python\n# torch/optim/_foreach.py\nfrom typing import Callable, cast, Optional, Union, Any\nimport torch\nfrom torch import Tensor\nfrom .optimizer import (\n    _to_scalar, _get_value, _view_as_real,\n    Optimizer, DeviceDict, DeviceDtypeDict\n)\n\n# Helper functions\ndef _foreach_apply_maximize(\n    device_grads: list[Tensor],\n    maximize: bool\n) -> list[Tensor]:\n    \"\"\"Apply maximize (negate grads) and return modified grads.\"\"\"\n    if maximize:\n        return torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n    return device_grads\n\ndef _foreach_apply_weight_decay(\n    device_grads: list[Tensor],\n    device_params: list[Tensor],\n    weight_decay: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    can_reuse_grads: bool = True\n) -> list[Tensor]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Apply weight decay to gradients with memory optimization.\n\n    When maximize has already allocated new memory for device_grads,\n    we can reuse that memory. Otherwise, allocate new memory.\n    \"\"\"\n    if weight_decay == 0:\n        return device_grads\n\n    # Reuse device_grads memory if it was already allocated for maximize\n    if can_reuse_grads and maximize:\n        torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n        return device_grads\n    else:\n        return torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n            device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay\n        )\n\ndef _foreach_optimizer_step(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    step_fn: Callable[..., None],\n    state_tensors: dict[str, list[Optional[Tensor]]],\n    has_sparse_grad: bool = False,\n    maximize: bool = False,\n    weight_decay: float = 0.0,\n    lr: Union[float, Tensor] = 1e-3,\n    **kwargs: Any\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Shared foreach optimizer step with common preprocessing.\n\n    Args:\n        params: List of parameters to optimize\n        grads: List of gradients\n        step_fn: Callback function that implements optimizer-specific logic\n        state_tensors: Dict mapping state names to lists of state tensors\n        has_sparse_grad: Whether sparse gradients are possible\n        maximize: Whether to maximize loss instead of minimize\n        weight_decay: Weight decay factor\n        lr: Learning rate\n        **kwargs: Additional arguments passed to step_fn\n    \"\"\"\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    # Group all tensors by device and dtype\n    tensors_to_group = [params, grads] + list(state_tensors.values())\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        tensors_to_group,\n        with_indices=True,\n    )\n\n    for grouped_tensors_tuple, indices in grouped_tensors.values():\n        # Extract grouped tensors\n        device_params_ = grouped_tensors_tuple[0]\n        device_grads_ = grouped_tensors_tuple[1]\n        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n\n        # Extract state tensors\n        device_states = {}\n        for i, (state_name, state_list) in enumerate(state_tensors.items(), start=2):\n            device_states[state_name] = cast(list[Optional[Tensor]], grouped_tensors_tuple[i])\n\n        # Check for sparse gradients in this device group\n        device_has_sparse_grad = has_sparse_grad and any(\n            grad.is_sparse for grad in device_grads\n        )\n\n        # Apply maximize\n        device_grads = _foreach_apply_maximize(device_grads, maximize)\n\n        # Apply weight decay\n        device_grads = _foreach_apply_weight_decay(\n            device_grads, device_params, weight_decay, maximize\n        )\n\n        # Call optimizer-specific step function\n        step_fn(\n            device_params=device_params,\n            device_grads=device_grads,\n            device_states=device_states,\n            device_has_sparse_grad=device_has_sparse_grad,\n            indices=indices,\n            lr=lr,\n            **kwargs\n        )\n```\n\n### Step 2: Refactor SGD's `_multi_tensor_sgd`\n\nReplace the common preprocessing with `_foreach_optimizer_step` while keeping momentum-specific logic.\n\n### Step 3: Refactor Adam's `_multi_tensor_adam`\n\nApply same pattern, keeping Adam-specific logic (step increment, bias correction, AMSGrad).\n\n### Step 4: Update Other Optimizers\n\nIncrementally refactor Adagrad, RMSprop, RAdam to use shared helpers.\n\n## Key Design Decisions\n\n1. **Callback approach**: Keep optimizer-specific logic in a step_fn callback to avoid forcing all optimizers into the same mold\n2. **Memory efficiency**: Maintain the existing memory-reuse optimization (e.g., reusing device_grads when maximize is applied)\n3. **Gradual refactoring**: Don't require all optimizers to change at once; allow phased migration\n4. **Backward compatibility**: Existing public APIs unchanged; only internal _multi_tensor_* functions refactored\n5. **Type safety**: Use cast() appropriately and preserve existing type hints\n\n## Testing Strategy\n\n1. Run existing optimizer tests: `python -m pytest test/test_optim.py -xvs`\n2. Verify numerical equivalence between old and new implementations\n3. Check that state_dict serialization still works\n4. Ensure hook APIs still fire in correct order\n5. Validate LR scheduler integration\n\n## Success Criteria Verification\n\n- [ ] `/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py` created with shared logic\n- [ ] At least 3 optimizer files import from _foreach (SGD, Adam, Adagrad)\n- [ ] Duplicate foreach patterns reduced (code duplications removed)\n- [ ] Original optimizer APIs still work (all tests pass)\n- [ ] No performance regressions\n\n## Critical Files\n\n### To Create:\n- `/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py`\n\n### To Modify:\n- `/workspace/torch/optim/sgd.py` (lines 378-471 of _multi_tensor_sgd)\n- `/workspace/torch/optim/adam.py` (lines 544-787 of _multi_tensor_adam)\n- `/workspace/torch/optim/adagrad.py` (lines ~388-495 of _multi_tensor_adagrad)\n\n### Utilities to Import:\nFrom `torch.optim.optimizer`:\n- `_to_scalar`, `_get_value`, `_view_as_real`\n- `Optimizer`, `DeviceDict`, `DeviceDtypeDict`\n\n## Implementation Notes\n\n1. The _foreach_optimizer_step function signature is flexible to accept any state tensors\n2. Each optimizer's step_fn is responsible for its own logic (momentum, exponential moving averages, etc.)\n3. The shared function handles the \"mechanical\" parts: grouping, maximize, weight_decay\n4. Complex dtype handling will need special treatment - may need helper or documented pattern\n5. Sparse gradient handling varies by optimizer - kept in step_fn for now\n\n## Status: READY FOR IMPLEMENTATION\nPlan is complete and ready for execution. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01MA6kBMvHEG19J9jtJ86Bbb","type":"tool_result","content":"The file /logs/agent/sessions/plans/lexical-chasing-kahn.md has been updated successfully."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"0b163590-219c-43a2-8e65-3b4605bcd762","uuid":"157d80e1-9043-44a9-bf16-ab5da028a1df","tool_use_result":{"type":"update","filePath":"/logs/agent/sessions/plans/lexical-chasing-kahn.md","content":"# Task: Extract Shared Foreach Optimizer Step\n\n## Context\nThe PyTorch optimizer implementations contain repeated `_multi_tensor_*` patterns across 11+ optimizer files. These functions have common preprocessing patterns that can be extracted to reduce duplication.\n\n## Exploration Results\n\n### Common Patterns Found Across _multi_tensor_* Functions\n1. **Empty parameter check**: All return early if `len(params) == 0`\n2. **LR scalar conversion**: `lr = _to_scalar(lr)`\n3. **Tensor grouping**: `Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype([...], with_indices=True)`\n4. **Maximize handling**: `if maximize: device_grads = torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)`\n5. **Weight decay**: Memory-optimized addition with reuse of intermediate tensors\n6. **Assertions**: `assert not differentiable` and `assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None`\n7. **Complex dtype handling**: `_view_as_real(...)` pattern\n8. **CPU step optimization**: Avoid repeated tensor wrapping for CPU-resident state\n\n### Utilities Available for Reuse\nFrom `torch.optim.optimizer`:\n- `_to_scalar()` - Convert tensor LR to scalar\n- `_get_value()` - Extract Python value from tensor\n- `_view_as_real()` - Convert complex tensors for processing\n- `_get_scalar_dtype()`, `_get_capturable_supported_devices()`\n- Type aliases: `DeviceDict`, `DeviceDtypeDict`\n- `Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype()` - Core grouping utility\n\n### Optimizer-Specific Differences\n- **SGD**: Momentum buffer handling, sparse gradient fallback\n- **Adam**: Parameter validation, step increment, decoupled weight decay, AMSGrad logic\n- **RMSprop**: Complex handling, centered variance, CPU step optimization\n- **Adagrad**: Sparse gradient fallback, LR decay per parameter\n- Each has distinct bias correction and state update logic that can't be fully abstracted\n\n## Implementation Strategy\n\n### Approach: Helper Functions + Callback-based Framework\n\nCreate `/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py` with:\n\n1. **Helper functions for common operations**:\n   - `_foreach_apply_maximize(device_grads, maximize)` - Negate gradients if maximizing\n   - `_foreach_apply_weight_decay(device_grads, device_params, weight_decay, maximize)` - Memory-optimized weight decay\n   - `_foreach_group_tensors_and_prepare(params, grads, state_tensors, maximize, weight_decay, **kwargs)` - Groups and preprocesses tensors\n\n2. **Main callback-based orchestration function**:\n   - `_foreach_optimizer_step(params, grads, step_fn, state_tensors, **kwargs)`\n   - Handles grouping, assertions, maximize, weight decay\n   - Calls `step_fn(device_params, device_grads, device_states, indices, **kwargs)` for optimizer-specific logic\n\n3. **Common utility functions**:\n   - `_get_group_device_dtype()` - Extract device/dtype from grouped tensors\n   - `_validate_foreach_hyperparams()` - Common validation for tensor hyperparameters\n\n### Files to Modify\n\n**Primary**:\n- Create: `/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py` with shared logic\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/sgd.py` - Use _foreach_optimizer_step\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/adam.py` - Use _foreach_optimizer_step\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/adamw.py` - Inherits from Adam (automatic)\n\n**Secondary** (to meet ≥3 file requirement + reduce duplication):\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/adagrad.py` - Use shared helpers\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/rmsprop.py` - Use shared helpers\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/radam.py` - Use shared helpers\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/adadelta.py` - Use shared helpers\n\n## Detailed Implementation Plan\n\n### Step 1: Create `torch/optim/_foreach.py`\n\n```python\n# torch/optim/_foreach.py\nfrom typing import Callable, cast, Optional, Union, Any\nimport torch\nfrom torch import Tensor\nfrom .optimizer import (\n    _to_scalar, _get_value, _view_as_real,\n    Optimizer, DeviceDict, DeviceDtypeDict\n)\n\n# Helper functions\ndef _foreach_apply_maximize(\n    device_grads: list[Tensor],\n    maximize: bool\n) -> list[Tensor]:\n    \"\"\"Apply maximize (negate grads) and return modified grads.\"\"\"\n    if maximize:\n        return torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n    return device_grads\n\ndef _foreach_apply_weight_decay(\n    device_grads: list[Tensor],\n    device_params: list[Tensor],\n    weight_decay: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    can_reuse_grads: bool = True\n) -> list[Tensor]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Apply weight decay to gradients with memory optimization.\n\n    When maximize has already allocated new memory for device_grads,\n    we can reuse that memory. Otherwise, allocate new memory.\n    \"\"\"\n    if weight_decay == 0:\n        return device_grads\n\n    # Reuse device_grads memory if it was already allocated for maximize\n    if can_reuse_grads and maximize:\n        torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n        return device_grads\n    else:\n        return torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n            device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay\n        )\n\ndef _foreach_optimizer_step(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    step_fn: Callable[..., None],\n    state_tensors: dict[str, list[Optional[Tensor]]],\n    has_sparse_grad: bool = False,\n    maximize: bool = False,\n    weight_decay: float = 0.0,\n    lr: Union[float, Tensor] = 1e-3,\n    **kwargs: Any\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Shared foreach optimizer step with common preprocessing.\n\n    Args:\n        params: List of parameters to optimize\n        grads: List of gradients\n        step_fn: Callback function that implements optimizer-specific logic\n        state_tensors: Dict mapping state names to lists of state tensors\n        has_sparse_grad: Whether sparse gradients are possible\n        maximize: Whether to maximize loss instead of minimize\n        weight_decay: Weight decay factor\n        lr: Learning rate\n        **kwargs: Additional arguments passed to step_fn\n    \"\"\"\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    # Group all tensors by device and dtype\n    tensors_to_group = [params, grads] + list(state_tensors.values())\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        tensors_to_group,\n        with_indices=True,\n    )\n\n    for grouped_tensors_tuple, indices in grouped_tensors.values():\n        # Extract grouped tensors\n        device_params_ = grouped_tensors_tuple[0]\n        device_grads_ = grouped_tensors_tuple[1]\n        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n\n        # Extract state tensors\n        device_states = {}\n        for i, (state_name, state_list) in enumerate(state_tensors.items(), start=2):\n            device_states[state_name] = cast(list[Optional[Tensor]], grouped_tensors_tuple[i])\n\n        # Check for sparse gradients in this device group\n        device_has_sparse_grad = has_sparse_grad and any(\n            grad.is_sparse for grad in device_grads\n        )\n\n        # Apply maximize\n        device_grads = _foreach_apply_maximize(device_grads, maximize)\n\n        # Apply weight decay\n        device_grads = _foreach_apply_weight_decay(\n            device_grads, device_params, weight_decay, maximize\n        )\n\n        # Call optimizer-specific step function\n        step_fn(\n            device_params=device_params,\n            device_grads=device_grads,\n            device_states=device_states,\n            device_has_sparse_grad=device_has_sparse_grad,\n            indices=indices,\n            lr=lr,\n            **kwargs\n        )\n```\n\n### Step 2: Refactor SGD's `_multi_tensor_sgd`\n\nReplace the common preprocessing with `_foreach_optimizer_step` while keeping momentum-specific logic.\n\n### Step 3: Refactor Adam's `_multi_tensor_adam`\n\nApply same pattern, keeping Adam-specific logic (step increment, bias correction, AMSGrad).\n\n### Step 4: Update Other Optimizers\n\nIncrementally refactor Adagrad, RMSprop, RAdam to use shared helpers.\n\n## Key Design Decisions\n\n1. **Callback approach**: Keep optimizer-specific logic in a step_fn callback to avoid forcing all optimizers into the same mold\n2. **Memory efficiency**: Maintain the existing memory-reuse optimization (e.g., reusing device_grads when maximize is applied)\n3. **Gradual refactoring**: Don't require all optimizers to change at once; allow phased migration\n4. **Backward compatibility**: Existing public APIs unchanged; only internal _multi_tensor_* functions refactored\n5. **Type safety**: Use cast() appropriately and preserve existing type hints\n\n## Testing Strategy\n\n1. Run existing optimizer tests: `python -m pytest test/test_optim.py -xvs`\n2. Verify numerical equivalence between old and new implementations\n3. Check that state_dict serialization still works\n4. Ensure hook APIs still fire in correct order\n5. Validate LR scheduler integration\n\n## Success Criteria Verification\n\n- [ ] `/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py` created with shared logic\n- [ ] At least 3 optimizer files import from _foreach (SGD, Adam, Adagrad)\n- [ ] Duplicate foreach patterns reduced (code duplications removed)\n- [ ] Original optimizer APIs still work (all tests pass)\n- [ ] No performance regressions\n\n## Critical Files\n\n### To Create:\n- `/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py`\n\n### To Modify:\n- `/workspace/torch/optim/sgd.py` (lines 378-471 of _multi_tensor_sgd)\n- `/workspace/torch/optim/adam.py` (lines 544-787 of _multi_tensor_adam)\n- `/workspace/torch/optim/adagrad.py` (lines ~388-495 of _multi_tensor_adagrad)\n\n### Utilities to Import:\nFrom `torch.optim.optimizer`:\n- `_to_scalar`, `_get_value`, `_view_as_real`\n- `Optimizer`, `DeviceDict`, `DeviceDtypeDict`\n\n## Implementation Notes\n\n1. Extract state tensors","+        device_states = {}","+        for i, (state_name, state_list) in enumerate(state_tensors.items(), start=2):","+            device_states[state_name] = cast(list[Optional[Tensor]], grouped_tensors_tuple[i])","+","+        # Validate LR scheduler integration","+","+## Success Criteria Verification","+","+- [ ] `/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py` created with shared logic","+- [ ] At least 3 optimizer files import from _foreach (SGD, Adam, Adagrad)","+- [ ] Duplicate foreach patterns reduced (code duplications removed)","+- [ ] Original optimizer APIs still work (all tests pass)","+- [ ] No performance regressions","+","+## Critical Files","+","+### To Create:","+- `/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py`","+","+### To Modify:","+- `/workspace/torch/optim/sgd.py` (lines 378-471 of _multi_tensor_sgd)","+- `/workspace/torch/optim/adam.py` (lines 544-787 of _multi_tensor_adam)","+- `/workspace/torch/optim/adagrad.py` (lines ~388-495 of _multi_tensor_adagrad)","+","+### Utilities to Import:","+From `torch.optim.optimizer`:","+- `_to_scalar`, `_get_value`, `_view_as_real`","+- `Optimizer`, `DeviceDict`, `DeviceDtypeDict`","+","+## Implementation Notes","+","+1. Check if params is empty\n2. Convert lr to scalar using `_to_scalar`\n3. Group tensors by device and dtype\n4. Apply maximize (negate gradients if maximize=True)\n5. Apply weight decay to gradients\n6. Handle the grouped tensors with optimizer-specific logic\n\n## Goal\nCreate a shared module `torch/optim/_foreach.py` that extracts these common patterns to reduce duplication across 11+ optimizer files while maintaining backward compatibility.\n\n## Key Questions to Explore\n1. What exact patterns are repeated across _multi_tensor_* functions?\n2. What's the best way to abstract the common logic without breaking the optimizer-specific implementations?\n3. Are there existing utilities we can reuse (e.g., from optimizer.py)?\n4. What testing approach should we use to verify the refactoring doesn't break anything?\n\n## Status: EXPLORING\nWill update after analyzing the codebase patterns.\n"}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01E14cWADumhHF1aH9xhJban","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01GVoUqPBrZcyyHL5x1TZZ9v","name":"ExitPlanMode","input":{"allowedPrompts":[{"tool":"Bash","prompt":"run tests to verify implementations"},{"tool":"Bash","prompt":"build and check compilation"}],"plan":"# Task: Extract Shared Foreach Optimizer Step\n\n## Context\nThe PyTorch optimizer implementations contain repeated `_multi_tensor_*` patterns across 11+ optimizer files. These functions have common preprocessing patterns that can be extracted to reduce duplication.\n\n## Exploration Results\n\n### Common Patterns Found Across _multi_tensor_* Functions\n1. **Empty parameter check**: All return early if `len(params) == 0`\n2. **LR scalar conversion**: `lr = _to_scalar(lr)`\n3. **Tensor grouping**: `Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype([...], with_indices=True)`\n4. **Maximize handling**: `if maximize: device_grads = torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)`\n5. **Weight decay**: Memory-optimized addition with reuse of intermediate tensors\n6. **Assertions**: `assert not differentiable` and `assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None`\n7. **Complex dtype handling**: `_view_as_real(...)` pattern\n8. **CPU step optimization**: Avoid repeated tensor wrapping for CPU-resident state\n\n### Utilities Available for Reuse\nFrom `torch.optim.optimizer`:\n- `_to_scalar()` - Convert tensor LR to scalar\n- `_get_value()` - Extract Python value from tensor\n- `_view_as_real()` - Convert complex tensors for processing\n- `_get_scalar_dtype()`, `_get_capturable_supported_devices()`\n- Type aliases: `DeviceDict`, `DeviceDtypeDict`\n- `Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype()` - Core grouping utility\n\n### Optimizer-Specific Differences\n- **SGD**: Momentum buffer handling, sparse gradient fallback\n- **Adam**: Parameter validation, step increment, decoupled weight decay, AMSGrad logic\n- **RMSprop**: Complex handling, centered variance, CPU step optimization\n- **Adagrad**: Sparse gradient fallback, LR decay per parameter\n- Each has distinct bias correction and state update logic that can't be fully abstracted\n\n## Implementation Strategy\n\n### Approach: Helper Functions + Callback-based Framework\n\nCreate `/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py` with:\n\n1. **Helper functions for common operations**:\n   - `_foreach_apply_maximize(device_grads, maximize)` - Negate gradients if maximizing\n   - `_foreach_apply_weight_decay(device_grads, device_params, weight_decay, maximize)` - Memory-optimized weight decay\n   - `_foreach_group_tensors_and_prepare(params, grads, state_tensors, maximize, weight_decay, **kwargs)` - Groups and preprocesses tensors\n\n2. **Main callback-based orchestration function**:\n   - `_foreach_optimizer_step(params, grads, step_fn, state_tensors, **kwargs)`\n   - Handles grouping, assertions, maximize, weight decay\n   - Calls `step_fn(device_params, device_grads, device_states, indices, **kwargs)` for optimizer-specific logic\n\n3. **Common utility functions**:\n   - `_get_group_device_dtype()` - Extract device/dtype from grouped tensors\n   - `_validate_foreach_hyperparams()` - Common validation for tensor hyperparameters\n\n### Files to Modify\n\n**Primary**:\n- Create: `/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py` with shared logic\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/sgd.py` - Use _foreach_optimizer_step\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/adam.py` - Use _foreach_optimizer_step\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/adamw.py` - Inherits from Adam (automatic)\n\n**Secondary** (to meet ≥3 file requirement + reduce duplication):\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/adagrad.py` - Use shared helpers\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/rmsprop.py` - Use shared helpers\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/radam.py` - Use shared helpers\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/adadelta.py` - Use shared helpers\n\n## Detailed Implementation Plan\n\n### Step 1: Create `torch/optim/_foreach.py`\n\n```python\n# torch/optim/_foreach.py\nfrom typing import Callable, cast, Optional, Union, Any\nimport torch\nfrom torch import Tensor\nfrom .optimizer import (\n    _to_scalar, _get_value, _view_as_real,\n    Optimizer, DeviceDict, DeviceDtypeDict\n)\n\n# Helper functions\ndef _foreach_apply_maximize(\n    device_grads: list[Tensor],\n    maximize: bool\n) -> list[Tensor]:\n    \"\"\"Apply maximize (negate grads) and return modified grads.\"\"\"\n    if maximize:\n        return torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n    return device_grads\n\ndef _foreach_apply_weight_decay(\n    device_grads: list[Tensor],\n    device_params: list[Tensor],\n    weight_decay: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    can_reuse_grads: bool = True\n) -> list[Tensor]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Apply weight decay to gradients with memory optimization.\n\n    When maximize has already allocated new memory for device_grads,\n    we can reuse that memory. Otherwise, allocate new memory.\n    \"\"\"\n    if weight_decay == 0:\n        return device_grads\n\n    # Reuse device_grads memory if it was already allocated for maximize\n    if can_reuse_grads and maximize:\n        torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n        return device_grads\n    else:\n        return torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n            device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay\n        )\n\ndef _foreach_optimizer_step(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    step_fn: Callable[..., None],\n    state_tensors: dict[str, list[Optional[Tensor]]],\n    has_sparse_grad: bool = False,\n    maximize: bool = False,\n    weight_decay: float = 0.0,\n    lr: Union[float, Tensor] = 1e-3,\n    **kwargs: Any\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Shared foreach optimizer step with common preprocessing.\n\n    Args:\n        params: List of parameters to optimize\n        grads: List of gradients\n        step_fn: Callback function that implements optimizer-specific logic\n        state_tensors: Dict mapping state names to lists of state tensors\n        has_sparse_grad: Whether sparse gradients are possible\n        maximize: Whether to maximize loss instead of minimize\n        weight_decay: Weight decay factor\n        lr: Learning rate\n        **kwargs: Additional arguments passed to step_fn\n    \"\"\"\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    # Group all tensors by device and dtype\n    tensors_to_group = [params, grads] + list(state_tensors.values())\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        tensors_to_group,\n        with_indices=True,\n    )\n\n    for grouped_tensors_tuple, indices in grouped_tensors.values():\n        # Extract grouped tensors\n        device_params_ = grouped_tensors_tuple[0]\n        device_grads_ = grouped_tensors_tuple[1]\n        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n\n        # Extract state tensors\n        device_states = {}\n        for i, (state_name, state_list) in enumerate(state_tensors.items(), start=2):\n            device_states[state_name] = cast(list[Optional[Tensor]], grouped_tensors_tuple[i])\n\n        # Check for sparse gradients in this device group\n        device_has_sparse_grad = has_sparse_grad and any(\n            grad.is_sparse for grad in device_grads\n        )\n\n        # Apply maximize\n        device_grads = _foreach_apply_maximize(device_grads, maximize)\n\n        # Apply weight decay\n        device_grads = _foreach_apply_weight_decay(\n            device_grads, device_params, weight_decay, maximize\n        )\n\n        # Call optimizer-specific step function\n        step_fn(\n            device_params=device_params,\n            device_grads=device_grads,\n            device_states=device_states,\n            device_has_sparse_grad=device_has_sparse_grad,\n            indices=indices,\n            lr=lr,\n            **kwargs\n        )\n```\n\n### Step 2: Refactor SGD's `_multi_tensor_sgd`\n\nReplace the common preprocessing with `_foreach_optimizer_step` while keeping momentum-specific logic.\n\n### Step 3: Refactor Adam's `_multi_tensor_adam`\n\nApply same pattern, keeping Adam-specific logic (step increment, bias correction, AMSGrad).\n\n### Step 4: Update Other Optimizers\n\nIncrementally refactor Adagrad, RMSprop, RAdam to use shared helpers.\n\n## Key Design Decisions\n\n1. **Callback approach**: Keep optimizer-specific logic in a step_fn callback to avoid forcing all optimizers into the same mold\n2. **Memory efficiency**: Maintain the existing memory-reuse optimization (e.g., reusing device_grads when maximize is applied)\n3. **Gradual refactoring**: Don't require all optimizers to change at once; allow phased migration\n4. **Backward compatibility**: Existing public APIs unchanged; only internal _multi_tensor_* functions refactored\n5. **Type safety**: Use cast() appropriately and preserve existing type hints\n\n## Testing Strategy\n\n1. Run existing optimizer tests: `python -m pytest test/test_optim.py -xvs`\n2. Verify numerical equivalence between old and new implementations\n3. Check that state_dict serialization still works\n4. Ensure hook APIs still fire in correct order\n5. Validate LR scheduler integration\n\n## Success Criteria Verification\n\n- [ ] `/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py` created with shared logic\n- [ ] At least 3 optimizer files import from _foreach (SGD, Adam, Adagrad)\n- [ ] Duplicate foreach patterns reduced (code duplications removed)\n- [ ] Original optimizer APIs still work (all tests pass)\n- [ ] No performance regressions\n\n## Critical Files\n\n### To Create:\n- `/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py`\n\n### To Modify:\n- `/workspace/torch/optim/sgd.py` (lines 378-471 of _multi_tensor_sgd)\n- `/workspace/torch/optim/adam.py` (lines 544-787 of _multi_tensor_adam)\n- `/workspace/torch/optim/adagrad.py` (lines ~388-495 of _multi_tensor_adagrad)\n\n### Utilities to Import:\nFrom `torch.optim.optimizer`:\n- `_to_scalar`, `_get_value`, `_view_as_real`\n- `Optimizer`, `DeviceDict`, `DeviceDtypeDict`\n\n## Implementation Notes\n\n1. The _foreach_optimizer_step function signature is flexible to accept any state tensors\n2. Each optimizer's step_fn is responsible for its own logic (momentum, exponential moving averages, etc.)\n3. The shared function handles the \"mechanical\" parts: grouping, maximize, weight_decay\n4. Complex dtype handling will need special treatment - may need helper or documented pattern\n5. Sparse gradient handling varies by optimizer - kept in step_fn for now\n\n## Status: READY FOR IMPLEMENTATION\nPlan is complete and ready for execution. User approval requested.\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":4,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3154,"cache_read_input_tokens":69646,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":3154},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"0b163590-219c-43a2-8e65-3b4605bcd762","uuid":"64f59541-7775-4535-bffe-a600b246fe9d"}

type: ignore[attr-defined]\n    return device_grads\n\n\ndef apply_weight_decay(\n    device_grads: list[Tensor],\n    device_params: list[Tensor],\n    weight_decay: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    can_reuse_grads: bool = True,\n) -> list[Tensor]:\n    \"\"\"Apply weight decay to gradients with memory optimization.\n\n    This function optimizes memory usage by reusing the device_grads allocation\n    when it has already been allocated for the maximize operation.\n\n    Args:\n        device_grads: List of gradients for a specific device/dtype group\n        device_params: List of parameters for the same device/dtype group\n        weight_decay: Weight decay coefficient\n        maximize: Whether maximize has already been applied (and allocated new memory)\n        can_reuse_grads: Whether the device_grads memory can be safely reused\n\n    Returns:\n        Modified gradient list with weight decay applied\n    \"\"\"\n    if weight_decay == 0:\n        return device_grads\n\n    # \"maximize\": maximize,\n            \"foreach\": foreach,\n            \"differentiable\": differentiable,\n            \"fused\": fused,\n        }\n        if nesterov and (momentum <= 0 or dampening != 0):\n            raise ValueError(\"Nesterov momentum requires a momentum and zero dampening\")\n        super().__init__(params, defaults)\n\n        if fused:\n
