group[\"capturable\"] or group[\"fused\"]\n                        else torch.tensor(0.0, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n                    )\n                    # Exponential moving average of gradient values\n                    state[\"exp_avg\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n                        p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n                    )\n                    # Exponential moving average of squared gradient values\n                    state[\"exp_avg_sq\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n                        p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n                    )\n                    if group[\"amsgrad\"]:\n                        # Maintains max of all exp. moving avg. of sq. grad. values\n                        state[\"max_exp_avg_sq\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n                            p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n                        )\n\n                exp_avgs.append(state[\"exp_avg\"])\n                exp_avg_sqs.append(state[\"exp_avg_sq\"])\n\n                if group[\"amsgrad\"]:\n                    max_exp_avg_sqs.append(state[\"max_exp_avg_sq\"])\n                if group[\"differentiable\"] and state[\"step\"].requires_grad:\n                    raise RuntimeError(\n                        \"`requires_grad` is not supported for `step` in differentiable mode\"\n                    )\n\n                # Foreach without capturable does not support a tensor lr\n                if (\n                    group[\"foreach\"]\n                    and torch.is_tensor(group[\"lr\"])\n                    and not group[\"capturable\"]\n                ):\n                    raise RuntimeError(\n                        \"lr as a Tensor is not supported for capturable=False and foreach=True\"\n                    )\n\n                state_steps.append(state[\"step\"])\n        return has_complex\n\n    @_use_grad_for_differentiable\n    def step(self, closure=None):\n        \"\"\"Perform a single optimization step.\n\n        Args:\n            closure (Callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model\n                and returns the loss.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._cuda_graph_capture_health_check()\n\n        loss = None\n        if closure is not None:\n            with torch.enable_grad():\n                loss = closure()\n\n        for group in self.param_groups:\n            params_with_grad: list[Tensor] = []\n            grads: list[Tensor] = []\n            exp_avgs: list[Tensor] = []\n            exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor] = []\n            max_exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor] = []\n            state_steps: list[Tensor] = []\n            beta1, beta2 = group[\"betas\"]\n\n            has_complex = self._init_group(\n                group,\n                params_with_grad,\n                grads,\n                exp_avgs,\n                exp_avg_sqs,\n                max_exp_avg_sqs,\n                state_steps,\n            )\n\n            adam(\n                params_with_grad,\n                grads,\n                exp_avgs,\n                exp_avg_sqs,\n                max_exp_avg_sqs,\n                state_steps,\n                amsgrad=group[\"amsgrad\"],\n                has_complex=has_complex,\n                beta1=beta1,\n                beta2=beta2,\n                lr=group[\"lr\"],\n                weight_decay=group[\"weight_decay\"],\n                eps=group[\"eps\"],\n                maximize=group[\"maximize\"],\n                foreach=group[\"foreach\"],\n                capturable=group[\"capturable\"],\n                differentiable=group[\"differentiable\"],\n                fused=group[\"fused\"],\n                grad_scale=getattr(self, \"grad_scale\", None),\n                found_inf=getattr(self, \"found_inf\", None),\n                decoupled_weight_decay=group[\"decoupled_weight_decay\"],\n            )\n\n        return loss\n\n\nAdam.__doc__ = (\n    r\"\"\"Implements Adam algorithm.\n\n    .. math::\n       \\begin{aligned}\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma \\text{ (lr)}, \\beta_1, \\beta_2\n                \\text{ (betas)},\\theta_0 \\text{ (params)},f(\\theta) \\text{ (objective)}          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{13mm}      \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)},  \\: \\textit{amsgrad},\n                \\:\\textit{maximize},  \\: \\epsilon \\text{ (epsilon)}                              \\\\\n            &\\textbf{initialize} :  m_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ ( first moment)},\n                v_0\\leftarrow 0 \\text{ (second moment)},\\: v_0^{max}\\leftarrow 0          \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{maximize}:                                       \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   -\\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})         \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{else}                                                           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda  \\theta_{t-1}                            \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}m_t           \\leftarrow   \\beta_1 m_{t-1} + (1 - \\beta_1) g_t          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}v_t           \\leftarrow   \\beta_2 v_{t-1} + (1-\\beta_2) g^2_t          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\widehat{m_t} \\leftarrow   m_t/\\big(1-\\beta_1^t \\big)                   \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: amsgrad                                                  \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm} v_t^{max} \\leftarrow \\mathrm{max}(v_{t-1}^{max},v_t)                  \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}\\widehat{v_t} \\leftarrow v_t^{max}/\\big(1-\\beta_2^t \\big)              \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{else}                                                           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}\\widehat{v_t} \\leftarrow   v_t/\\big(1-\\beta_2^t \\big)                  \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1} - \\gamma \\widehat{m_t}/\n                \\big(\\sqrt{\\widehat{v_t}} + \\epsilon \\big)                                       \\\\\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n       \\end{aligned}\n\n    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to `Adam: A Method for Stochastic Optimization`_.\n    \"\"\"\n    + rf\"\"\"\n    Args:\n        {_params_doc}\n        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-3). A tensor LR\n            is not yet supported for all our implementations. Please use a float\n            LR if you are not also specifying fused=True or capturable=True.\n        betas (Tuple[float, float], optional): coefficients used for computing\n            running averages of gradient and its square (default: (0.9, 0.999))\n        eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve\n            numerical stability (default: 1e-8)\n        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n        decoupled_weight_decay (bool, optional): if True, this optimizer is\n            equivalent to AdamW and the algorithm will not accumulate weight\n            decay in the momentum nor variance. (default: False)\n        amsgrad (bool, optional): whether to use the AMSGrad variant of this\n            algorithm from the paper `On the Convergence of Adam and Beyond`_\n            (default: False)\n        {_foreach_doc}\n        {_maximize_doc}\n        {_capturable_doc}\n        {_differentiable_doc}\n        {_fused_doc}\n    .. Note::\n        A prototype implementation of Adam and AdamW for MPS supports `torch.float32` and `torch.float16`.\n    .. _Adam\\: A Method for Stochastic Optimization:\n        https://arxiv.org/abs/1412.6980\n    .. _On the Convergence of Adam and Beyond:\n        https://openreview.net/forum?id=ryQu7f-RZ\n\n    \"\"\"\n)\n\n\ndef _single_tensor_adam(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n    max_exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    amsgrad: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n    beta1: Union[float, Tensor],\n    beta2: Union[float, Tensor],\n    lr: Union[float, Tensor],\n    weight_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    capturable: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    decoupled_weight_decay: bool,\n):\n    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n\n    if torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        # this assert is due to JIT being dumb and not realizing that the ops below\n        # have overloads to handle both float and Tensor lrs, so we just assert it's\n        # a float since most people using JIT are using floats\n        assert isinstance(lr, float)\n        assert isinstance(beta1, float)\n        assert isinstance(beta2, float)\n    else:\n        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n        # TODO: Support nonzero-dim Tensor betas, see #147921\n\n    # We only shuffle around the beta when it is a Tensor, otherwise, we prefer\n    # treating it as a scalar.\n    # Note: ensure type declaration is under conditional check for isinstance\n    # or else torchscript will get cranky about the DeviceDict type.\n    if isinstance(beta1, Tensor):\n        beta1_dict: Optional[DeviceDtypeDict] = {(beta1.device, beta1.dtype): beta1}\n    else:\n        beta1_dict = None\n\n    for i, param in enumerate(params):\n        grad = grads[i] if not maximize else -grads[i]\n        exp_avg = exp_avgs[i]\n        exp_avg_sq = exp_avg_sqs[i]\n        step_t = state_steps[i]\n\n        # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n            capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices()\n            assert (\n                param.device.type == step_t.device.type\n                and param.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n            ), (\n                f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n            )\n\n        # update step\n        step_t += 1\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            if decoupled_weight_decay:\n                # Perform stepweight decay\n                param.mul_(1 - lr * weight_decay)\n            else:\n                # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules\n                if differentiable and isinstance(weight_decay, Tensor):\n                    if weight_decay.requires_grad:\n                        grad = grad.addcmul_(param.clone(), weight_decay)\n                    else:\n                        grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n                else:\n                    grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n\n        if torch.is_complex(param):\n            grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n            exp_avg = torch.view_as_real(exp_avg)\n            exp_avg_sq = torch.view_as_real(exp_avg_sq)\n            if amsgrad:\n                max_exp_avg_sqs[i] = torch.view_as_real(max_exp_avg_sqs[i])\n            param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n\n        device = param.device\n\n        if beta1_dict is not None:\n            dtype = param.dtype  # type: ignore[union-attr]\n\n            # cast to workaround https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/140601\n            key = (device, dtype)\n            if key not in beta1_dict:\n                beta1_dict[key] = beta1.to(  # type: ignore[union-attr]\n                    device=device, dtype=dtype, non_blocking=True\n                )\n\n            device_beta1: Union[float, Tensor] = beta1_dict[key]\n        else:\n            device_beta1 = beta1\n\n        # Decay the first and second moment running average coefficient\n        exp_avg.lerp_(grad, 1 - device_beta1)\n\n        # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules\n        if differentiable and isinstance(beta2, Tensor):\n            if beta2.requires_grad:\n                # Using lerp to only use 2 operations bc addcmul's value cannot be a tensor\n                # Showing equivalence of differentiable path and nondifferentiable path\n                # expavg * b2 + grad^2 * (1-b2)\n                #           add expavg * (1-b2) - expavg * (1-b2) = 0\n                # expavg * b2 + expavg * (1-b2) - expavg * (1-b2) + grad^2 * (1-b2)\n                # expavg - expavg * (1-b2) + grad^2 * (1-b2)\n                # expavg + (grad^2 - expavg) * (1-b2)\n                # expavg.lerp(grad^2, 1-beta2)\n                exp_avg_sq.lerp_(torch.square(grad), weight=1 - beta2)\n            else:\n                exp_avg_sq.mul_(beta2).addcmul_(\n                    grad, grad, value=cast(float, 1 - beta2)\n                )\n        else:\n            exp_avg_sq.mul_(beta2).addcmul_(grad, grad, value=1 - beta2)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n\n        if capturable or differentiable:\n            step = step_t\n\n            # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules\n            if differentiable and isinstance(beta1, Tensor):\n                if beta1.requires_grad:\n                    bias_correction1 = 1 - beta1 ** step.clone()\n                else:\n                    bias_correction1 = 1 - beta1**step\n            else:\n                bias_correction1 = 1 - beta1**step\n\n            # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules\n            if differentiable and isinstance(beta2, Tensor):\n                if beta2.requires_grad:\n                    bias_correction2 = 1 - beta2 ** step.clone()\n                else:\n                    bias_correction2 = 1 - beta2**step\n            else:\n                bias_correction2 = 1 - beta2**step\n\n            step_size = lr / bias_correction1\n            step_size_neg = step_size.neg()\n\n            bias_correction2_sqrt = bias_correction2.sqrt()\n\n            if amsgrad:\n                # Maintains the maximum of all 2nd moment running avg. till now\n                if differentiable:\n                    max_exp_avg_sq = max_exp_avg_sqs[i].clone()\n                else:\n                    max_exp_avg_sq = max_exp_avg_sqs[i]\n\n                max_exp_avg_sqs[i].copy_(torch.maximum(max_exp_avg_sq, exp_avg_sq))\n\n                # Uses the max. for normalizing running avg. of gradient\n                # Folds in (admittedly ugly) 1-elem step_size math here to avoid extra param-set-sized read+write\n                # (can't fold it into addcdiv_ below because addcdiv_ requires value is a Number, not a Tensor)\n                denom = (\n                    max_exp_avg_sqs[i].sqrt() / (bias_correction2_sqrt * step_size_neg)\n                ).add_(eps / step_size_neg)\n            else:\n                denom = (\n                    exp_avg_sq.sqrt() / (bias_correction2_sqrt * step_size_neg)\n                ).add_(eps / step_size_neg)\n\n            if differentiable:\n                param.addcdiv_(exp_avg.clone(), denom)\n            else:\n                param.addcdiv_(exp_avg, denom)\n        else:\n            step = _get_value(step_t)\n\n            bias_correction1 = 1 - beta1**step\n            bias_correction2 = 1 - beta2**step\n\n            step_size = lr / bias_correction1\n\n            bias_correction2_sqrt = bias_correction2**0.5\n\n            if amsgrad:\n                # Maintains the maximum of all 2nd moment running avg. till now\n                torch.maximum(max_exp_avg_sqs[i], exp_avg_sq, out=max_exp_avg_sqs[i])\n\n                # Use the max. for normalizing running avg. of gradient\n                denom = (max_exp_avg_sqs[i].sqrt() / bias_correction2_sqrt).add_(eps)\n            else:\n                denom = (exp_avg_sq.sqrt() / bias_correction2_sqrt).add_(eps)\n\n            param.addcdiv_(exp_avg, denom, value=-step_size)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n\n        # Lastly, switch back to complex view\n        if amsgrad and torch.is_complex(params[i]):\n            max_exp_avg_sqs[i] = torch.view_as_complex(max_exp_avg_sqs[i])\n\n\ndef _multi_tensor_adam(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n    max_exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    amsgrad: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n    beta1: Union[float, Tensor],\n    beta2: Union[float, Tensor],\n    lr: Union[float, Tensor],\n    weight_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    capturable: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    decoupled_weight_decay: bool,\n):\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    if isinstance(lr, Tensor):\n        if not capturable:\n            raise RuntimeError(\n                \"lr as a Tensor is not supported for capturable=False and foreach=True\"\n            )\n        if lr.numel() != 1:\n            raise ValueError(\"Tensor lr must be 1-element\")\n\n    if isinstance(beta1, Tensor):\n        if not capturable:\n            raise ValueError(\n                \"beta1 as a Tensor is not supported for capturable=False and foreach=True\"\n            )\n        if beta1.numel() != 1:\n            raise ValueError(\"Tensor beta1 must be 1-element\")\n\n    if isinstance(beta2, Tensor):\n        if not capturable:\n            raise ValueError(\n                \"beta2 as a Tensor is not supported for capturable=False and foreach=True\"\n            )\n        if beta2.numel() != 1:\n            raise ValueError(\"Tensor beta2 must be 1-element\")\n\n    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(\n            supports_xla=False\n        )\n        assert all(\n            p.device.type == step.device.type\n            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n            for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)\n        ), (\n            f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n        )\n\n    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n\n    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n    # TODO: Support nonzero-dim Tensor betas, see #147921\n\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, exp_avgs, exp_avg_sqs, max_exp_avg_sqs, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n\n    # We only shuffle around the beta when it is a Tensor and on CUDA, otherwise, we prefer\n    # treating it as a scalar.\n    beta1_dict: Optional[DeviceDict] = (  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n        {beta1.device: beta1}\n        if isinstance(beta1, Tensor) and str(beta1.device) != \"cpu\"\n        else None\n    )\n\n    for (\n        device_params_,\n        device_grads_,\n        device_exp_avgs_,\n        device_exp_avg_sqs_,\n        device_max_exp_avg_sqs_,\n        device_state_steps_,\n    ), _ in grouped_tensors.values():\n        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n        device_exp_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], device_exp_avgs_)\n        device_exp_avg_sqs = cast(list[Tensor], device_exp_avg_sqs_)\n        device_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_steps_)\n\n        device = device_params[0].device\n        if beta1_dict is not None and device not in beta1_dict:\n            beta1_dict[device] = beta1.to(device=device, non_blocking=True)  # type: ignore[union-attr, attr-defined]\n\n        device_beta1 = beta1_dict[device] if beta1_dict else beta1\n\n        # Handle complex parameters\n        if has_complex:\n            if amsgrad:\n                device_max_exp_avg_sqs = cast(list[Tensor], device_max_exp_avg_sqs_)\n                _view_as_real(\n                    device_params,\n                    device_grads,\n                    device_exp_avgs,\n                    device_exp_avg_sqs,\n                    device_max_exp_avg_sqs,\n                )\n            else:\n                _view_as_real(\n                    device_params, device_grads, device_exp_avgs, device_exp_avg_sqs\n                )\n\n        if maximize:\n            device_grads = torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        # Update steps\n        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and device_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n            torch._foreach_add_(\n                device_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n            )\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            if decoupled_weight_decay:\n                # Perform stepweight decay\n                torch._foreach_mul_(device_params, 1 - lr * weight_decay)\n            else:\n                # Reuse the intermediate memory (device_grads) already allocated for maximize\n                if maximize:\n                    torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n                else:\n                    device_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n                        device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay\n                    )\n\n        # Decay the first and second moment running average coefficient\n        # Use device beta1 if beta1 is a tensor to ensure all\n        # tensors are on the same device\n        torch._foreach_lerp_(\n            device_exp_avgs, device_grads, cast(float, 1 - device_beta1)\n        )\n\n        torch._foreach_mul_(device_exp_avg_sqs, beta2)\n\n        # Due to the strictness of the _foreach_addcmul API, we can't have a single\n        # tensor scalar as the scalar arg (only python number is supported there)\n        # as a result, separate out the value mul\n        # Filed https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/139795\n        if isinstance(beta2, torch.Tensor):\n            scaled_device_grads = torch._foreach_mul(device_grads, 1 - beta2)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n            value = 1.0\n        else:\n            scaled_device_grads = device_grads  # type: ignore[assignment]\n            value = 1 - beta2\n\n        torch._foreach_addcmul_(\n            device_exp_avg_sqs, scaled_device_grads, device_grads, value\n        )\n\n        # Delete the local intermediate(s) since they won't be used anymore to save on peak memory\n        del device_grads\n        del scaled_device_grads\n\n        bias_correction1: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n        bias_correction2: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n        bias_correction2_sqrt: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n\n        if capturable:\n            bias_correction1 = torch._foreach_pow(beta1, device_state_steps)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n            bias_correction2 = torch._foreach_pow(beta2, device_state_steps)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n            # foreach_sub doesn't allow a scalar as the first arg\n            torch._foreach_sub_(bias_correction1, 1)\n            torch._foreach_sub_(bias_correction2, 1)\n            # we do not negate bias_correction1 as it'll need to be negated later anyway\n            torch._foreach_neg_(bias_correction2)\n\n            # foreach_div doesn't allow a scalar as the first arg\n            torch._foreach_div_(bias_correction1, lr)\n            torch._foreach_reciprocal_(bias_correction1)\n\n            torch._foreach_sqrt_(bias_correction2)\n\n            # Re-assign for clarity as we maintain minimal intermediates: we'll have\n            # step_size = - lr / (1 - beta1 ^ t) where t = num_steps\n            # bias_correction2_sqrt = sqrt(1 - beta2 ^ t)\n            step_size = bias_correction1\n            bias_correction2_sqrt = bias_correction2\n\n            if amsgrad:\n                device_max_exp_avg_sqs = cast(list[Tensor], device_max_exp_avg_sqs_)\n                # Maintains the maximum of all 2nd moment running avg. till now\n                torch._foreach_maximum_(device_max_exp_avg_sqs, device_exp_avg_sqs)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n                # Set intermediate to the max. for normalizing running avg. of gradient when amsgrad\n                exp_avg_sq_sqrt = torch._foreach_sqrt(device_max_exp_avg_sqs)\n            else:\n                exp_avg_sq_sqrt = torch._foreach_sqrt(device_exp_avg_sqs)\n\n            torch._foreach_div_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, bias_correction2_sqrt)\n            torch._foreach_add_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, eps)\n            torch._foreach_div_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, step_size)\n\n            # at this point, exp_avg_sq_sqrt = - (1 - beta^t) * [sqrt(exp_avg_sq / (1 - beta2^t)) + eps] / lr\n            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(device_params, device_exp_avgs, exp_avg_sq_sqrt)\n        else:\n            bias_correction1 = [\n                1 - beta1 ** _get_value(step) for step in device_state_steps\n            ]\n            bias_correction2 = [\n                1 - beta2 ** _get_value(step) for step in device_state_steps\n            ]\n\n            step_size = _stack_if_compiling([(lr / bc) * -1 for bc in bias_correction1])\n\n            bias_correction2_sqrt = [bc**0.5 for bc in bias_correction2]  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n\n            if amsgrad:\n                device_max_exp_avg_sqs = cast(list[Tensor], device_max_exp_avg_sqs_)\n                # Maintains the maximum of all 2nd moment running avg. till now\n                torch._foreach_maximum_(device_max_exp_avg_sqs, device_exp_avg_sqs)\n\n                # Use the max. for normalizing running avg. of gradient\n                exp_avg_sq_sqrt = torch._foreach_sqrt(device_max_exp_avg_sqs)\n            else:\n                exp_avg_sq_sqrt = torch._foreach_sqrt(device_exp_avg_sqs)\n\n            torch._foreach_div_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, bias_correction2_sqrt)\n            torch._foreach_add_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, eps)\n            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(\n                device_params,\n                device_exp_avgs,\n                exp_avg_sq_sqrt,\n                step_size,  #             \\\\\n            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma \\text{ (lr)}, \\: \\theta_0 \\text{ (params)}, \\: f(\\theta)\n                \\text{ (objective)}, \\: \\lambda \\text{ (weight          &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n       \\end{aligned}\n\n    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to `Adaptive 1e-2)\n        lr_decay (float, optional): learning rate decay (default: 0)\n        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n        initial_accumulator_value (float, optional): initial value of the\n            sum of squares of gradients (default: 0)\n        eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve\n            numerical stability (default: 1e-10)\n        {_foreach_doc}\n        {_maximize_doc}\n        {_differentiable_doc}\n        fused (bool, optional): whether the fused implementation (CPU only) is used.\n            Currently, `torch.float64`, `torch.float32`, `torch.float16`, and `torch.bfloat16`\n            are supported. (default: None). Please note that the fused implementations does not\n            support sparse or complex gradients.\n    .. _Adaptive Subgradient Methods for Online Learning and Stochastic\n        Optimization: http://jmlr.org/papers/v12/duchi11a.html\n\n    \"\"\"\n)\n\n\ndef adagrad(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    state_sums: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    fused: Optional[bool] = None,\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n    # setting these as kwargs for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n    has_sparse_grad: bool = False,\n    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n    differentiable: bool = False,\n    has_complex: bool = False,\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    lr_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n):\n    r\"\"\"Functional API that performs Adagrad algorithm computation.\n\n    See :class:`~torch.optim.Adagrad` for details.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not all(isinstance(t, torch.Tensor) for t in state_steps):\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            \"API has changed, `state_steps` argument must contain a list of singleton tensors\"\n        )\n\n    # Respect when the user inputs False/True for foreach or fused. We only want to change\n    # the default when neither have been user-specified. Note that we default to foreach\n    # and pass False to use_fused. This is not a mistake--we want to give the fused impl\n    # bake-in time before making it the default, even if it is typically faster.\n    if fused is None and foreach is None:\n        _, foreach = _default_to_fused_or_foreach(\n            params, differentiable, use_fused=False\n        )\n\n    if fused is None:\n        fused = False\n    if foreach is None:\n        foreach = False\n\n    if foreach and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers\")\n    if fused and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with fused optimizers\")\n\n    if fused and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        func = _fused_adagrad\n    elif foreach and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        func = _multi_tensor_adagrad\n    else:\n        func = _single_tensor_adagrad\n\n    func(\n        params,\n        grads,\n        state_sums,\n        state_steps,\n        lr=lr,\n        weight_decay=weight_decay,\n        lr_decay=lr_decay,\n        eps=eps,\n        has_sparse_grad=has_sparse_grad,\n        maximize=maximize,\n        differentiable=differentiable,\n        has_complex=has_complex,\n        grad_scale=grad_scale,\n        found_inf=found_inf,\n    )\n\n\ndef _make_sparse(grad, grad_indices, values):\n    size = grad.size()\n    return torch.sparse_coo_tensor(grad_indices, values, size)\n\n\ndef _single_tensor_adagrad(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    state_sums: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    lr_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    has_sparse_grad: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n\n    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    for param, grad, state_sum, step_t in zip(params, grads, state_sums, state_steps):\n        # update step\n        step_t += 1\n        step = _get_value(step_t)\n        grad = grad if not maximize else -grad\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            if grad.is_sparse:\n                raise RuntimeError(\n                    \"weight_decay option is not compatible with sparse gradients\"\n                )\n            grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n\n        clr = lr / (1 + (step - 1) * lr_decay)\n\n        if grad.is_sparse:\n            grad = grad.coalesce()  # the update is non-linear so indices must be unique\n            grad_indices = grad._indices()\n            grad_values = grad._values()\n\n            state_sum.add_(_make_sparse(grad, grad_indices, grad_values.pow(2)))\n            std = state_sum.sparse_mask(grad)\n            std_values = std._values().sqrt_().add_(eps)\n            param.add_(\n                _make_sparse(grad, grad_indices, grad_values / std_values), alpha=-clr\n            )\n        else:\n            is_complex = torch.is_complex(param)\n            if is_complex:\n                grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n                state_sum = torch.view_as_real(state_sum)\n                param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n            state_sum.addcmul_(grad, grad, value=1)\n            if differentiable:\n                std = state_sum.sqrt() + eps\n            else:\n                std = state_sum.sqrt().add_(eps)\n            param.addcdiv_(grad, std, value=-clr)\n            if is_complex:\n                param = torch.view_as_complex(param)\n                state_sum = torch.view_as_complex(state_sum)\n\n\ndef _multi_tensor_adagrad(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    state_sums: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    lr_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    has_sparse_grad: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n\n    # Foreach functions will throw errors if given empty lists\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    grouped_tensorlists = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, state_sums, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n    for (\n        device_params_,\n        device_grads_,\n        device_state_sums_,\n        device_state_steps_,\n    ), _ in grouped_tensorlists.values():\n        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n        device_state_sums = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_sums_)\n        device_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_steps_)\n\n        device_has_sparse_grad = has_sparse_grad and any(\n            grad.is_sparse for grad in device_grads\n        )\n\n        if device_has_sparse_grad:\n            _single_tensor_adagrad(\n                device_params,\n                device_grads,\n                device_state_sums,\n                device_state_steps,\n                lr=lr,\n                weight_decay=weight_decay,\n                lr_decay=lr_decay,\n                eps=eps,\n                has_sparse_grad=True,\n                maximize=maximize,\n                differentiable=differentiable,\n                has_complex=has_complex,\n                grad_scale=grad_scale,\n                found_inf=found_inf,\n            )\n            continue\n\n        # Handle complex parameters\n        if has_complex:\n            _view_as_real(device_params, device_grads, device_state_sums)\n\n        if maximize:\n            device_grads = torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        # Update steps\n        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and device_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n            torch._foreach_add_(\n                device_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n            )\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            # Reuse the intermediate memory (device_grads) already allocated for maximize\n            if maximize:\n                torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n            else:\n                device_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n                    device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay\n                )\n\n        minus_clr = [\n            -lr / (1 + (_get_value(step) - 1) * lr_decay) for step in device_state_steps\n        ]\n\n        torch._foreach_addcmul_(device_state_sums, device_grads, device_grads, value=1)\n\n        std = torch._foreach_sqrt(device_state_sums)\n        torch._foreach_add_(std, eps)\n\n        if weight_decay != 0 or maximize:\n            # Again, reuse the intermediate memory (device_grads) already allocated\n            torch._foreach_mul_(device_grads, minus_clr)\n            numerator = device_grads\n        else:\n            numerator = torch._foreach_mul(device_grads, minus_clr)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        torch._foreach_addcdiv_(device_params, numerator, std)\n\n\ndef _fused_adagrad(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    state_sums: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    lr_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    has_sparse_grad: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n) -> None:\n    if not params:\n        return\n    if has_sparse_grad or has_complex:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"`fused` does not support sparse grad or complex param\")\n\n    if differentiable:\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            \"adagrad with fused=True does not support differentiable=True\"\n        )\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    grad_scale_dict = (\n        {grad_scale.device: grad_scale} if grad_scale is not None else None\n    )\n    found_inf_dict = {found_inf.device: found_inf} if found_inf is not None else None\n\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, state_sums, state_steps]  #                                          \\\\\n            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma \\text{ (lr)}, \\: \\theta_0 \\text{ (params)}, \\: f(\\theta)\n                \\text{ (objective)}, \\: \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)},                          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{12mm}    \\tau \\text{ (initial accumulator value)}, \\: \\eta\\text{ (lr decay)}\\\\\n            &\\textbf{initialize} :  state\\_sum_0 \\leftarrow \\tau                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm} \\tilde{\\gamma}    \\leftarrow \\gamma / (1 +(t-1) \\eta)                  \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm} \\textbf{if} \\: \\lambda \\neq 0      \\\\\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n       \\end{aligned}\n\n    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to `Adaptive Subgradient Methods for Online Learning\n    and Stochastic Optimization`_.\n    \"\"\"\n    + rf\"\"\"\n    Args:\n        {_params_doc}\n        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-2)\n        lr_decay (float, optional): learning rate decay (default: 0)\n        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n        initial_accumulator_value (float, optional): initial value of the\n            sum of squares of gradients (default: 0)\n        eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve\n            numerical stability (default: 1e-10)\n        {_foreach_doc}\n        {_maximize_doc}\n        {_differentiable_doc}\n        fused (bool, optional): whether the fused implementation (CPU only) is used.\n            Currently, `torch.float64`, `torch.float32`, `torch.float16`, and `torch.bfloat16`\n            are supported. (default: None). Please note that the fused implementations does not\n            support sparse or complex gradients.\n    .. _Adaptive Subgradient Methods for Online Learning and Stochastic\n        Optimization: http://jmlr.org/papers/v12/duchi11a.html\n\n    \"\"\"\n)\n\n\ndef adagrad(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    state_sums: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    fused: Optional[bool] = None,\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n    # setting these as kwargs for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n    has_sparse_grad: bool = False,\n    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n    differentiable: bool = False,\n    has_complex: bool = False,\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    lr_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n):\n    r\"\"\"Functional API that performs Adagrad algorithm computation.\n\n    See :class:`~torch.optim.Adagrad` for details.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not all(isinstance(t, torch.Tensor) for t in state_steps):\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            \"API has changed, `state_steps` argument must contain a list of singleton tensors\"\n        )\n\n    # Respect when the user inputs False/True for foreach or fused. We only want to change\n    # the default when neither have been user-specified. Note that we default to foreach\n    # and pass False to use_fused. This is not a mistake--we want to give the fused impl\n    # bake-in time before making it the default, even if it is typically faster.\n    if fused is None and foreach is None:\n        _, foreach = _default_to_fused_or_foreach(\n            params, differentiable, use_fused=False\n        )\n\n    if fused is None:\n        fused = False\n    if foreach is None:\n        foreach = False\n\n    if foreach and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers\")\n    if fused and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with fused optimizers\")\n\n    if fused and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        func = _fused_adagrad\n    elif foreach and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        func = _multi_tensor_adagrad\n    else:\n        func = _single_tensor_adagrad\n\n    func(\n        params,\n        grads,\n        state_sums,\n        state_steps,\n        lr=lr,\n        weight_decay=weight_decay,\n        lr_decay=lr_decay,\n        eps=eps,\n        has_sparse_grad=has_sparse_grad,\n        maximize=maximize,\n        differentiable=differentiable,\n        has_complex=has_complex,\n        grad_scale=grad_scale,\n        found_inf=found_inf,\n    )\n\n\ndef _make_sparse(grad, grad_indices, values):\n    size = grad.size()\n    return torch.sparse_coo_tensor(grad_indices, values, size)\n\n\ndef _single_tensor_adagrad(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    state_sums: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    lr_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    has_sparse_grad: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n\n    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    for param, grad, state_sum, step_t in zip(params, grads, state_sums, state_steps):\n        # update step\n        step_t += 1\n        step = _get_value(step_t)\n        grad = grad if not maximize else -grad\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            if grad.is_sparse:\n                raise RuntimeError(\n                    \"weight_decay option is not compatible with sparse gradients\"\n                )\n            grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n\n        clr = lr / (1 + (step - 1) * lr_decay)\n\n        if grad.is_sparse:\n            grad = grad.coalesce()  # the update is non-linear so indices must be unique\n            grad_indices = grad._indices()\n            grad_values = grad._values()\n\n            state_sum.add_(_make_sparse(grad, grad_indices, grad_values.pow(2)))\n            std = state_sum.sparse_mask(grad)\n            std_values = std._values().sqrt_().add_(eps)\n            param.add_(\n                _make_sparse(grad, grad_indices, grad_values / std_values), alpha=-clr\n            )\n        else:\n            is_complex = torch.is_complex(param)\n            if is_complex:\n                grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n                state_sum = torch.view_as_real(state_sum)\n                param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n            state_sum.addcmul_(grad, grad, value=1)\n            if differentiable:\n                std = state_sum.sqrt() + eps\n            else:\n                std = state_sum.sqrt().add_(eps)\n            param.addcdiv_(grad, std, value=-clr)\n            if is_complex:\n                param = torch.view_as_complex(param)\n                state_sum = torch.view_as_complex(state_sum)\n\n\ndef _multi_tensor_adagrad(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    state_sums: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    lr_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    has_sparse_grad: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n\n    # Foreach functions will throw errors if given empty lists\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    grouped_tensorlists = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, state_sums, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n    for (\n        device_params_,\n        device_grads_,\n        device_state_sums_,\n        device_state_steps_,\n    ), _ in grouped_tensorlists.values():\n        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n        device_state_sums = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_sums_)\n        device_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_steps_)\n\n        device_has_sparse_grad = has_sparse_grad and any(\n            grad.is_sparse for grad in device_grads\n        )\n\n        if device_has_sparse_grad:\n differentiable=differentiable,\n                has_complex=has_complex,\n                grad_scale=grad_scale,\n                found_inf=found_inf,\n            )\n            continue\n\n        # Handle complex parameters\n        if has_complex:\n            _view_as_real(device_params, device_grads, device_state_sums)\n\n        if maximize:\n            device_grads = torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        # Update steps\n        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and device_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n            torch._foreach_add_(\n                device_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n            )\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            # Reuse the intermediate memory (device_grads) already allocated for maximize\n            if maximize:\n                torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n            else:\n                device_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n                    device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay\n                )\n\n        minus_clr = [\n            -lr / (1 + (_get_value(step) - 1) * lr_decay) for step in device_state_steps\n        ]\n\n        torch._foreach_addcmul_(device_state_sums, device_grads, device_grads, value=1)\n\n        std = torch._foreach_sqrt(device_state_sums)\n        torch._foreach_add_(std, eps)\n\n        if weight_decay != 0 or maximize:\n            # Again, reuse the intermediate memory (device_grads) already allocated\n            torch._foreach_mul_(device_grads, minus_clr)\n            numerator = device_grads\n        else:\n            numerator = torch._foreach_mul(device_grads, minus_clr)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        torch._foreach_addcdiv_(device_params, numerator, std)\n\n\ndef _fused_adagrad(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    state_sums: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    lr_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    has_sparse_grad: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n) -> None:\n    if not params:\n        return\n    if has_sparse_grad or has_complex:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"`fused` does not support sparse grad or complex param\")\n\n    if differentiable:\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            \"adagrad with fused=True does not support differentiable=True\"\n        )\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    grad_scale_dict = (\n        {grad_scale.device: grad_scale} if grad_scale is not None else None\n    )\n    found_inf_dict = {found_inf.device: found_inf} if found_inf is not None else None\n\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, state_sums, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n    for (device, _), (\n        (\n            device_params_,\n            device_grads_,\n            device_state_sums_,\n            device_state_steps_,\n        ),\n        _,\n    ) in grouped_tensors.items():\n        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n        device_state_sums = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_sums_)\n        device_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_steps_)\n\n        device_grad_scale, device_found_inf = None, None\n        if grad_scale is not None and grad_scale_dict is not None:\n            if device not in grad_scale_dict:\n                grad_scale_dict[device] = grad_scale.to(device, non_blocking=True)  # type: ignore[index]\n            device_grad_scale = grad_scale_dict[device]  # type: ignore[index]\n        if found_inf is not None and found_inf_dict is not None:\n            if found_inf not in found_inf_dict:\n                found_inf_dict[device] = found_inf.to(device, non_blocking=True)  # type: ignore[index]\n            device_found_inf = found_inf_dict[device]  # type: ignore[index]\n        torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n        torch._fused_adagrad_(\n            device_params,\n            device_grads,\n            device_state_sums,\n            device_state_steps,\n            lr=lr,\n            lr_decay=lr_decay,\n            weight_decay=weight_decay,\n            eps=eps,\n            maximize=maximize,\n            grad_scale=device_grad_scale,\n            found_inf=device_found_inf,\n        )\n        if device_found_inf is not None:\n            torch._foreach_sub_(\n                device_state_steps, [device_found_inf] * len(device_state_steps)\n            )\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":453,"oldLines":8,"newStart":453,"newLines":7,"lines":["         if has_complex:","             _view_as_real(device_params, device_grads, device_state_sums)"," ","-        if maximize:","-            device_grads = torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01EN2FtiwUyjamTdBfSL1jo7","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_016dBey76VWB6Ni6uL2UdQEa","name":"ExitPlanMode","input":{"plan":"# Task: Extract Shared Foreach Optimizer Step\n\n## Context\nThe PyTorch optimizer implementations contain repeated `_multi_tensor_*` patterns across 11+ optimizer files. These functions have common preprocessing patterns that can be extracted to reduce duplication.\n\n## Exploration Results\n\n### Common Patterns Found Across _multi_tensor_* Functions\n1. **Empty parameter check**: All return early if `len(params) == 0`\n2. **LR scalar conversion**: `lr = _to_scalar(lr)`\n3. **Tensor grouping**: `Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype([...], with_indices=True)`\n4. **Maximize handling**: `if maximize: device_grads = torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)`\n5. **Weight decay**: Memory-optimized addition with reuse of intermediate tensors\n6. **Assertions**: `assert not differentiable` and `assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None`\n7. **Complex dtype handling**: `_view_as_real(...)` pattern\n8. **CPU step optimization**: Avoid repeated tensor wrapping for CPU-resident state\n\n### Utilities Available for Reuse\nFrom `torch.optim.optimizer`:\n- `_to_scalar()` - Convert tensor LR to scalar\n- `_get_value()` - Extract Python value from tensor\n- `_view_as_real()` - Convert complex tensors for processing\n- `_get_scalar_dtype()`, `_get_capturable_supported_devices()`\n- Type aliases: `DeviceDict`, `DeviceDtypeDict`\n- `Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype()` - Core grouping utility\n\n### Optimizer-Specific Differences\n- **SGD**: Momentum buffer handling, sparse gradient fallback\n- **Adam**: Parameter validation, step increment, decoupled weight decay, AMSGrad logic\n- **RMSprop**: Complex handling, centered variance, CPU step optimization\n- **Adagrad**: Sparse gradient fallback, LR decay per parameter\n- Each has distinct bias correction and state update logic that can't be fully abstracted\n\n## Implementation Strategy\n\n### Approach: Helper Functions + Callback-based Framework\n\nCreate `/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py` with:\n\n1. **Helper functions for common operations**:\n   - `_foreach_apply_maximize(device_grads, maximize)` - Negate gradients if maximizing\n   - `_foreach_apply_weight_decay(device_grads, device_params, weight_decay, maximize)` - Memory-optimized weight decay\n   - `_foreach_group_tensors_and_prepare(params, grads, state_tensors, maximize, weight_decay, **kwargs)` - Groups and preprocesses tensors\n\n2. **Main callback-based orchestration function**:\n   - `_foreach_optimizer_step(params, grads, step_fn, state_tensors, **kwargs)`\n   - Handles grouping, assertions, maximize, weight decay\n   - Calls `step_fn(device_params, device_grads, device_states, indices, **kwargs)` for optimizer-specific logic\n\n3. **Common utility functions**:\n   - `_get_group_device_dtype()` - Extract device/dtype from grouped tensors\n   - `_validate_foreach_hyperparams()` - Common validation for tensor hyperparameters\n\n### Files to Modify\n\n**Primary**:\n- Create: `/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py` with shared logic\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/sgd.py` - Use _foreach_optimizer_step\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/adam.py` - Use _foreach_optimizer_step\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/adamw.py` - Inherits from Adam (automatic)\n\n**Secondary** (to meet ≥3 file requirement + reduce duplication):\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/adagrad.py` - Use shared helpers\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/rmsprop.py` - Use shared helpers\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/radam.py` - Use shared helpers\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/adadelta.py` - Use shared helpers\n\n## Detailed Implementation Plan\n\n### Step 1: Create `torch/optim/_foreach.py`\n\n```python\n# torch/optim/_foreach.py\nfrom typing import Callable, cast, Optional, Union, Any\nimport torch\nfrom torch import Tensor\nfrom .optimizer import (\n    _to_scalar, _get_value, _view_as_real,\n    Optimizer, DeviceDict, DeviceDtypeDict\n)\n\n# Helper functions\ndef _foreach_apply_maximize(\n    device_grads: list[Tensor],\n    maximize: bool\n) -> list[Tensor]:\n    \"\"\"Apply maximize (negate grads) and return modified grads.\"\"\"\n    if maximize:\n        return torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n    return device_grads\n\ndef _foreach_apply_weight_decay(\n    device_grads: list[Tensor],\n    device_params: list[Tensor],\n    weight_decay: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    can_reuse_grads: bool = True\n) -> list[Tensor]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Apply weight decay to gradients with memory optimization.\n\n    When maximize has already allocated new memory for device_grads,\n    we can reuse that memory. Otherwise, allocate new memory.\n    \"\"\"\n    if weight_decay == 0:\n        return device_grads\n\n    # Reuse device_grads memory if it was already allocated for maximize\n    if can_reuse_grads and maximize:\n        torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n        return device_grads\n    else:\n        return torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n            device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay\n        )\n\ndef _foreach_optimizer_step(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    step_fn: Callable[..., None],\n    state_tensors: dict[str, list[Optional[Tensor]]],\n    has_sparse_grad: bool = False,\n    maximize: bool = False,\n    weight_decay: float = 0.0,\n    lr: Union[float, Tensor] = 1e-3,\n    **kwargs: Any\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Shared foreach optimizer step with common preprocessing.\n\n    Args:\n        params: List of parameters to optimize\n        grads: List of gradients\n        step_fn: Callback function that implements optimizer-specific logic\n        state_tensors: Dict mapping state names to lists of state tensors\n        has_sparse_grad: Whether sparse gradients are possible\n        maximize: Whether to maximize loss instead of minimize\n        weight_decay: Weight decay factor\n        lr: Learning rate\n        **kwargs: Additional arguments passed to step_fn\n    \"\"\"\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    # Group all tensors by device and dtype\n    tensors_to_group = [params, grads] + list(state_tensors.values())\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        tensors_to_group,\n        with_indices=True,\n    )\n\n    for grouped_tensors_tuple, indices in grouped_tensors.values():\n        # Extract grouped tensors\n        device_params_ = grouped_tensors_tuple[0]\n        device_grads_ = grouped_tensors_tuple[1]\n        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n\n        # Extract state tensors\n        device_states = {}\n        for i, (state_name, state_list) in enumerate(state_tensors.items(), start=2):\n            device_states[state_name] = cast(list[Optional[Tensor]], grouped_tensors_tuple[i])\n\n        # Check for sparse gradients in this device group\n        device_has_sparse_grad = has_sparse_grad and any(\n            grad.is_sparse for grad in device_grads\n        )\n\n        # Apply maximize\n        device_grads = _foreach_apply_maximize(device_grads, maximize)\n\n        # Apply weight decay\n        device_grads = _foreach_apply_weight_decay(\n            device_grads, device_params, weight_decay, maximize\n        )\n\n        # Call optimizer-specific step function\n        step_fn(\n            device_params=device_params,\n            device_grads=device_grads,\n            device_states=device_states,\n            device_has_sparse_grad=device_has_sparse_grad,\n            indices=indices,\n            lr=lr,\n            **kwargs\n        )\n```\n\n### Step 2: Refactor SGD's `_multi_tensor_sgd`\n\nReplace the common preprocessing with `_foreach_optimizer_step` while keeping momentum-specific logic.\n\n### Step 3: Refactor Adam's `_multi_tensor_adam`\n\nApply same pattern, keeping Adam-specific logic (step increment, bias correction, AMSGrad).\n\n### Step 4: Update Other Optimizers\n\nIncrementally refactor Adagrad, RMSprop, RAdam to use shared helpers.\n\n## Key Design Decisions\n\n1. **Callback approach**: Keep optimizer-specific logic in a step_fn callback to avoid forcing all optimizers into the same mold\n2. **Memory efficiency**: Maintain the existing memory-reuse optimization (e.g., reusing device_grads when maximize is applied)\n3. **Gradual refactoring**: Don't require all optimizers to change at once; allow phased migration\n4. **Backward compatibility**: Existing public APIs unchanged; only internal _multi_tensor_* functions refactored\n5. **Type safety**: Use cast() appropriately and preserve existing type hints\n\n## Testing Strategy\n\n1. Run existing optimizer tests: `python -m pytest test/test_optim.py -xvs`\n2. Verify numerical equivalence between old and new implementations\n3. Check that state_dict serialization still works\n4. Ensure hook APIs still fire in correct order\n5. Validate LR scheduler integration\n\n## Success Criteria Verification\n\n- [ ] `/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py` created with shared logic\n- [ ] At least 3 optimizer files import from _foreach (SGD, Adam, Adagrad)\n- [ ] Duplicate foreach patterns reduced (code duplications removed)\n- [ ] Original optimizer APIs still work (all tests pass)\n- [ ] No performance regressions\n\n## Critical Files\n\n### To Create:\n- `/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py`\n\n### To Modify:\n- `/workspace/torch/optim/sgd.py` (lines 378-471 of _multi_tensor_sgd)\n- `/workspace/torch/optim/adam.py` (lines 544-787 of _multi_tensor_adam)\n- `/workspace/torch/optim/adagrad.py` (lines ~388-495 of _multi_tensor_adagrad)\n\n### Utilities to Import:\nFrom `torch.optim.optimizer`:\n- `_to_scalar`, `_get_value`, `_view_as_real`\n- `Optimizer`, `DeviceDict`, `DeviceDtypeDict`\n\n## Implementation Notes\n\n1. The _foreach_optimizer_step function signature is flexible to accept any state tensors\n2. Each optimizer's step_fn is responsible for its own logic (momentum, exponential moving averages, etc.)\n3. The shared function handles the \"mechanical\" parts: grouping, maximize, weight_decay\n4. Complex dtype handling will need special treatment - may need helper or documented pattern\n5. Sparse gradient handling varies by optimizer - kept in step_fn for now\n\n## Status: READY FOR IMPLEMENTATION\nPlan is complete and ready for execution. These functions have common preprocessing patterns that can be extracted to reduce duplication.\n\n## Exploration Results\n\n### Common Patterns Found Across _multi_tensor_* Functions\n1. **Empty parameter check**: All return early if `len(params) == 0`\n2. **LR scalar conversion**: `lr = _to_scalar(lr)`\n3. **Tensor grouping**: `Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype([...], with_indices=True)`\n4. **Maximize handling**: `if maximize: device_grads = torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)`\n5. **Weight decay**: Memory-optimized addition with reuse of intermediate tensors\n6. **Assertions**: `assert not differentiable` and `assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None`\n7. **Complex dtype handling**: `_view_as_real(...)` pattern\n8. **CPU step optimization**: Avoid repeated tensor wrapping for CPU-resident state\n\n### Utilities Available for Reuse\nFrom `torch.optim.optimizer`:\n- `_to_scalar()` - Convert tensor LR to scalar\n- `_get_value()` - Extract Python value from tensor\n- `_view_as_real()` - Convert complex tensors for processing\n- `_get_scalar_dtype()`, `_get_capturable_supported_devices()`\n- Type aliases: `DeviceDict`, `DeviceDtypeDict`\n- `Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype()` - Core grouping utility\n\n### Optimizer-Specific Differences\n- **SGD**: Momentum buffer handling, sparse gradient fallback\n- **Adam**: Parameter validation, step increment, decoupled weight decay, AMSGrad logic\n- **RMSprop**: Complex handling, centered variance, CPU step optimization\n- **Adagrad**: Sparse gradient fallback, LR decay per parameter\n- Each has distinct bias correction and state update logic that can't be fully abstracted\n\n## Implementation Strategy\n\n### Approach: Helper Functions + Callback-based Framework\n\nCreate `/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py` with:\n\n1. **Helper functions for common operations**:\n   - `_foreach_apply_maximize(device_grads, maximize)` - Negate gradients if maximizing\n   - `_foreach_apply_weight_decay(device_grads, device_params, weight_decay, maximize)` - Memory-optimized weight decay\n   - `_foreach_group_tensors_and_prepare(params, grads, state_tensors, maximize, weight_decay, **kwargs)` - Groups and preprocesses tensors\n\n2. **Main callback-based orchestration function**:\n   - `_foreach_optimizer_step(params, grads, step_fn, state_tensors, **kwargs)`\n   - Handles grouping, assertions, maximize, weight decay\n   - Calls `step_fn(device_params, device_grads, device_states, indices, **kwargs)` for optimizer-specific logic\n\n3. **Common utility functions**:\n   - `_get_group_device_dtype()` - Extract device/dtype from grouped tensors\n   - `_validate_foreach_hyperparams()` - Common validation for tensor hyperparameters\n\n### Files to Modify\n\n**Primary**:\n- Create: `/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py` with shared logic\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/sgd.py` - Use _foreach_optimizer_step\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/adam.py` - Use _foreach_optimizer_step\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/adamw.py` - Inherits from Adam (automatic)\n\n**Secondary** (to meet ≥3 file requirement + reduce duplication):\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/adagrad.py` - Use shared helpers\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/rmsprop.py` - Use shared helpers\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/radam.py` - Use shared helpers\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/adadelta.py` - Use shared helpers\n\n## Detailed Implementation Plan\n\n### Step 1: Create `torch/optim/_foreach.py`\n\n```python\n# torch/optim/_foreach.py\nfrom typing import Callable, cast, Optional, Union, Any\nimport torch\nfrom torch import Tensor\nfrom .optimizer import (\n    _to_scalar, _get_value, _view_as_real,\n    Optimizer, DeviceDict, DeviceDtypeDict\n)\n\n# Helper functions\ndef _foreach_apply_maximize(\n    device_grads: list[Tensor],\n    maximize: bool\n) -> list[Tensor]:\n    \"\"\"Apply maximize (negate grads) and return modified grads.\"\"\"\n    if maximize:\n        return torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n    return device_grads\n\ndef _foreach_apply_weight_decay(\n    device_grads: list[Tensor],\n    device_params: list[Tensor],\n    weight_decay: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    can_reuse_grads: bool = True\n) -> list[Tensor]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Apply weight decay to gradients with memory optimization.\n\n    When maximize has already allocated new memory for device_grads,\n    we can reuse that memory. Otherwise, allocate new memory.\n    \"\"\"\n    if weight_decay == 0:\n        return device_grads\n\n    # Reuse device_grads memory if it was already allocated for maximize\n    if can_reuse_grads and maximize:\n        torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n        return device_grads\n    else:\n        return torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n            device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay\n        )\n\ndef _foreach_optimizer_step(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    step_fn: Callable[..., None],\n    state_tensors: dict[str, list[Optional[Tensor]]],\n    has_sparse_grad: bool = False,\n    maximize: bool = False,\n    weight_decay: float = 0.0,\n    lr: Union[float, Tensor] = 1e-3,\n    **kwargs: Any\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Shared foreach optimizer step with common preprocessing.\n\n    Args:\n        params: List of parameters to optimize\n        grads: List of gradients\n        step_fn: Callback function that implements optimizer-specific logic\n        state_tensors: Dict mapping state names to lists of state tensors\n        has_sparse_grad: Whether sparse gradients are possible\n        maximize: Whether to maximize loss instead of minimize\n        weight_decay: Weight decay factor\n        lr: Learning rate\n        **kwargs: Additional arguments passed to step_fn\n    \"\"\"\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    # Group all tensors by device and dtype\n    tensors_to_group = [params, grads] + list(state_tensors.values())\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        tensors_to_group,\n        with_indices=True,\n    )\n\n    for grouped_tensors_tuple, indices in grouped_tensors.values():\n        # Extract grouped tensors\n        device_params_ = grouped_tensors_tuple[0]\n        device_grads_ = grouped_tensors_tuple[1]\n        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n\n        # Extract state tensors\n        device_states = {}\n        for i, (state_name, state_list) in enumerate(state_tensors.items(), start=2):\n            device_states[state_name] = cast(list[Optional[Tensor]], grouped_tensors_tuple[i])\n\n        # Check for sparse gradients in this device group\n        device_has_sparse_grad = has_sparse_grad and any(\n            grad.is_sparse for grad in device_grads\n        )\n\n        # Apply maximize\n        device_grads = _foreach_apply_maximize(device_grads, maximize)\n\n        # Apply weight decay\n        device_grads = _foreach_apply_weight_decay(\n            device_grads, device_params, weight_decay, maximize\n        )\n\n        # Call optimizer-specific step function\n        step_fn(\n            device_params=device_params,\n            device_grads=device_grads,\n            device_states=device_states,\n            device_has_sparse_grad=device_has_sparse_grad,\n            indices=indices,\n            lr=lr,\n            **kwargs\n        )\n```\n\n### Step 2: Refactor SGD's `_multi_tensor_sgd`\n\nReplace the common preprocessing with `_foreach_optimizer_step` while keeping momentum-specific logic.\n\n### Step 3: Refactor Adam's `_multi_tensor_adam`\n\nApply same pattern, keeping Adam-specific logic (step increment, bias correction, AMSGrad).\n\n### Step 4: Update Other Optimizers\n\nIncrementally refactor Adagrad, RMSprop, RAdam to use shared helpers.\n\n## Key Design Decisions\n\n1. **Callback approach**: Keep optimizer-specific logic in a step_fn callback to avoid forcing all optimizers into the same mold\n2. **Memory efficiency**: Maintain the existing memory-reuse optimization (e.g., reusing device_grads when maximize is applied)\n3. **Gradual refactoring**: Don't require all optimizers to change at once; allow phased migration\n4. **Backward compatibility**: Existing public APIs unchanged; only internal _multi_tensor_* functions refactored\n5. **Type safety**: Use cast() appropriately and preserve existing type hints\n\n## Testing Strategy\n\n1. Run existing optimizer tests: `python -m pytest test/test_optim.py -xvs`\n2. Verify numerical equivalence between old and new implementations\n3. Check that state_dict serialization still works\n4. Ensure hook APIs still fire in correct order\n5. Validate LR scheduler integration\n\n## Success Criteria Verification\n\n- [ ] `/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py` created with shared logic\n- [ ] At least 3 optimizer files import from _foreach (SGD, Adam, Adagrad)\n- [ ] Duplicate foreach patterns reduced (code duplications removed)\n- [ ] Original optimizer APIs still work (all tests pass)\n- [ ] No performance regressions\n\n## Critical Files\n\n### To Create:\n- `/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py`\n\n### To Modify:\n- `/workspace/torch/optim/sgd.py` (lines 378-471 of _multi_tensor_sgd)\n- `/workspace/torch/optim/adam.py` (lines 544-787 of _multi_tensor_adam)\n- `/workspace/torch/optim/adagrad.py` (lines ~388-495 of _multi_tensor_adagrad)\n\n### Utilities to Import:\nFrom `torch.optim.optimizer`:\n- `_to_scalar`, `_get_value`, `_view_as_real`\n- `Optimizer`, `DeviceDict`, `DeviceDtypeDict`\n\n## Implementation Notes\n\n1. The _foreach_optimizer_step function signature is flexible to accept any state tensors\n2. Each optimizer's step_fn is responsible for its own logic (momentum, exponential moving averages, etc.)\n3. The shared function handles the \"mechanical\" parts: grouping, maximize, weight_decay\n4. Complex dtype handling will need special treatment - may need helper or documented pattern\n5. Sparse gradient handling varies by optimizer - kept in step_fn for now\n\n## Status: READY FOR IMPLEMENTATION\nPlan is complete and ready for execution. User approval requested.\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":247,"oldLines":5,"newStart":247,"newLines":46,"lines":[" 4.
