tests to verify implementations"},{"tool":"Bash","prompt":"build and check compilation"}],"plan":"# Task: Extract Shared Foreach Optimizer Step\n\n## Context\nThe PyTorch optimizer implementations contain repeated `_multi_tensor_*` patterns across 11+ optimizer files. These functions have common preprocessing patterns that can be extracted to reduce duplication.\n\n## Exploration Results\n\n### Common Patterns Found Across _multi_tensor_* Functions\n1. **Empty parameter check**: All return early if `len(params) == 0`\n2. **LR scalar conversion**: `lr = _to_scalar(lr)`\n3. **Tensor grouping**: `Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype([...], with_indices=True)`\n4. **Maximize handling**: `if maximize: device_grads = torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)`\n5. **Weight decay**: Memory-optimized addition with reuse of intermediate tensors\n6. **Assertions**: `assert not differentiable` and `assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None`\n7. **Complex dtype handling**: `_view_as_real(...)` pattern\n8. **CPU step optimization**: Avoid repeated tensor wrapping for CPU-resident state\n\n### Utilities Available for Reuse\nFrom `torch.optim.optimizer`:\n- `_to_scalar()` - Convert tensor LR to scalar\n- `_get_value()` - Extract Python value from tensor\n- `_view_as_real()` - Convert complex tensors for processing\n- `_get_scalar_dtype()`, `_get_capturable_supported_devices()`\n- Type aliases: `DeviceDict`, `DeviceDtypeDict`\n- `Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype()` - Core grouping utility\n\n### Optimizer-Specific Differences\n- **SGD**: Momentum buffer handling, sparse gradient fallback\n- **Adam**: Parameter validation, step increment, decoupled weight decay, AMSGrad logic\n- **RMSprop**: Complex handling, centered variance, CPU step optimization\n- **Adagrad**: Sparse gradient fallback, LR decay per parameter\n- Each has distinct bias correction and state update logic that can't be fully abstracted\n\n## Implementation Strategy\n\n### Approach: Helper Functions + Callback-based Framework\n\nCreate `/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py` with:\n\n1. **Helper functions for common operations**:\n   - `_foreach_apply_maximize(device_grads, maximize)` - Negate gradients if maximizing\n   - `_foreach_apply_weight_decay(device_grads, device_params, weight_decay, maximize)` - Memory-optimized weight decay\n   - `_foreach_group_tensors_and_prepare(params, grads, state_tensors, maximize, weight_decay, **kwargs)` - Groups and preprocesses tensors\n\n2. **Main callback-based orchestration function**:\n   - `_foreach_optimizer_step(params, grads, step_fn, state_tensors, **kwargs)`\n   - Handles grouping, assertions, maximize, weight decay\n   - Calls `step_fn(device_params, device_grads, device_states, indices, **kwargs)` for optimizer-specific logic\n\n3. **Common utility functions**:\n   - `_get_group_device_dtype()` - Extract device/dtype from grouped tensors\n   - `_validate_foreach_hyperparams()` - Common validation for tensor hyperparameters\n\n### Files to Modify\n\n**Primary**:\n- Create: `/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py` with shared logic\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/sgd.py` - Use _foreach_optimizer_step\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/adam.py` - Use _foreach_optimizer_step\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/adamw.py` - Inherits from Adam (automatic)\n\n**Secondary** (to meet ≥3 file requirement + reduce duplication):\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/adagrad.py` - Use shared helpers\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/rmsprop.py` - Use shared helpers\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/radam.py` - Use shared helpers\n- Update: `/workspace/torch/optim/adadelta.py` - Use shared helpers\n\n## Detailed Implementation Plan\n\n### Step 1: Create `torch/optim/_foreach.py`\n\n```python\n# torch/optim/_foreach.py\nfrom typing import Callable, cast, Optional, Union, Any\nimport torch\nfrom torch import Tensor\nfrom .optimizer import (\n    _to_scalar, _get_value, _view_as_real,\n    Optimizer, DeviceDict, DeviceDtypeDict\n)\n\n# Helper functions\ndef _foreach_apply_maximize(\n    device_grads: list[Tensor],\n    maximize: bool\n) -> list[Tensor]:\n    \"\"\"Apply maximize (negate grads) and return modified grads.\"\"\"\n    if maximize:\n        return torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n    return device_grads\n\ndef _foreach_apply_weight_decay(\n    device_grads: list[Tensor],\n    device_params: list[Tensor],\n    weight_decay: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    can_reuse_grads: bool = True\n) -> list[Tensor]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Apply weight decay to gradients with memory optimization.\n\n    When maximize has already allocated new memory for device_grads,\n    we can reuse that memory. Otherwise, allocate new memory.\n    \"\"\"\n    if weight_decay == 0:\n        return device_grads\n\n    # Reuse device_grads memory if it was already allocated for maximize\n    if can_reuse_grads and maximize:\n        torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n        return device_grads\n    else:\n        return torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n            device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay\n        )\n\ndef _foreach_optimizer_step(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    step_fn: Callable[..., None],\n    state_tensors: dict[str, list[Optional[Tensor]]],\n    has_sparse_grad: bool = False,\n    maximize: bool = False,\n    weight_decay: float = 0.0,\n    lr: Union[float, Tensor] = 1e-3,\n    **kwargs: Any\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Shared foreach optimizer step with common preprocessing.\n\n    Args:\n        params: List of parameters to optimize\n        grads: List of gradients\n        step_fn: Callback function that implements optimizer-specific logic\n        state_tensors: Dict mapping state names to lists of state tensors\n        has_sparse_grad: Whether sparse gradients are possible\n        maximize: Whether to maximize loss instead of minimize\n        weight_decay: Weight decay factor\n        lr: Learning rate\n        **kwargs: Additional arguments passed to step_fn\n    \"\"\"\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    # Group all tensors by device and dtype\n    tensors_to_group = [params, grads] + list(state_tensors.values())\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        tensors_to_group,\n        with_indices=True,\n    )\n\n    for grouped_tensors_tuple, indices in grouped_tensors.values():\n        # Extract grouped tensors\n        device_params_ = grouped_tensors_tuple[0]\n        device_grads_ = grouped_tensors_tuple[1]\n        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n\n        # Extract state tensors\n        device_states = {}\n        for i, (state_name, state_list) in enumerate(state_tensors.items(), start=2):\n            device_states[state_name] = cast(list[Optional[Tensor]], grouped_tensors_tuple[i])\n\n        # Check for sparse gradients in this device group\n        device_has_sparse_grad = has_sparse_grad and any(\n            grad.is_sparse for grad in device_grads\n        )\n\n        # Apply maximize\n        device_grads = _foreach_apply_maximize(device_grads, maximize)\n\n        # Apply weight decay\n        device_grads = _foreach_apply_weight_decay(\n            device_grads, device_params, weight_decay, maximize\n        )\n\n        # Call optimizer-specific step function\n        step_fn(\n            device_params=device_params,\n            device_grads=device_grads,\n            device_states=device_states,\n            device_has_sparse_grad=device_has_sparse_grad,\n            indices=indices,\n            lr=lr,\n            **kwargs\n        )\n```\n\n### Step 2: Refactor SGD's `_multi_tensor_sgd`\n\nReplace the common preprocessing with `_foreach_optimizer_step` while keeping momentum-specific logic.\n\n### Step 3: Refactor Adam's `_multi_tensor_adam`\n\nApply same pattern, keeping Adam-specific logic (step increment, bias correction, AMSGrad).\n\n### Step 4: Update Other Optimizers\n\nIncrementally refactor Adagrad, RMSprop, RAdam to use shared helpers.\n\n## Key Design Decisions\n\n1. **Callback approach**: Keep optimizer-specific logic in a step_fn callback to avoid forcing all optimizers into the same mold\n2. **Memory efficiency**: Maintain the existing memory-reuse optimization (e.g., reusing device_grads when maximize is applied)\n3. **Gradual refactoring**: Don't require all optimizers to change at once; allow phased migration\n4. **Backward compatibility**: Existing public APIs unchanged; only internal _multi_tensor_* functions refactored\n5. **Type safety**: Use cast() appropriately and preserve existing type hints\n\n## Testing Strategy\n\n1. Run existing optimizer tests: `python -m pytest test/test_optim.py -xvs`\n2. Verify numerical equivalence between old and new implementations\n3. Check that state_dict serialization still works\n4. Ensure hook APIs still fire in correct order\n5. Validate LR scheduler integration\n\n## Success Criteria Verification\n\n- [ ] `/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py` created with shared logic\n- [ ] At least 3 optimizer files import from _foreach (SGD, Adam, Adagrad)\n- [ ] Duplicate foreach patterns reduced (code duplications removed)\n- [ ] Original optimizer APIs still work (all tests pass)\n- [ ] No performance regressions\n\n## Critical Files\n\n### To Create:\n- `/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py`\n\n### To Modify:\n- `/workspace/torch/optim/sgd.py` (lines 378-471 of _multi_tensor_sgd)\n- `/workspace/torch/optim/adam.py` (lines 544-787 of _multi_tensor_adam)\n- `/workspace/torch/optim/adagrad.py` (lines ~388-495 of _multi_tensor_adagrad)\n\n### Utilities to Import:\nFrom `torch.optim.optimizer`:\n- `_to_scalar`, `_get_value`, `_view_as_real`\n- `Optimizer`, `DeviceDict`, `DeviceDtypeDict`\n\n## Implementation Notes\n\n1. The _foreach_optimizer_step function signature is flexible to accept any state tensors\n2. Each optimizer's step_fn is responsible for its own logic (momentum, exponential moving averages, etc.)\n3. The shared function handles the \"mechanical\" parts: grouping, maximize, weight_decay\n4. Complex dtype handling will need special treatment - may need helper or documented pattern\n5. Sparse gradient handling varies by optimizer - kept in step_fn for now\n\n## Status: READY FOR IMPLEMENTATION\nPlan is complete and ready for execution. User approval requested.\n"}},{"tool_name":"ExitPlanMode","tool_use_id":"toolu_016dBey76VWB6Ni6uL2UdQEa","tool_input":{"plan":"# Task: Extract Shared Foreach Optimizer Step\n\n## Context\nThe PyTorch optimizer implementations contain repeated `_multi_tensor_*` patterns across 11+ optimizer files. Use shared helpers\n\n## Detailed Implementation Plan\n\n### Step 1: Create `torch/optim/_foreach.py`\n\n```python\n# torch/optim/_foreach.py\nfrom typing import Callable, cast, Optional, Union, Any\nimport torch\nfrom torch import Tensor\nfrom .optimizer import (\n    _to_scalar, _get_value, _view_as_real,\n    Optimizer, DeviceDict, DeviceDtypeDict\n)\n\n# Helper functions\ndef _foreach_apply_maximize(\n    device_grads: list[Tensor],\n    maximize: bool\n) -> list[Tensor]:\n    \"\"\"Apply maximize (negate grads) and return modified grads.\"\"\"\n    if maximize:\n        return torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n    return device_grads\n\ndef _foreach_apply_weight_decay(\n    device_grads: list[Tensor],\n    device_params: list[Tensor],\n    weight_decay: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    can_reuse_grads: bool = True\n) -> list[Tensor]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Apply weight decay to gradients with memory optimization.\n\n    When maximize has already allocated new memory for device_grads,\n    we can reuse that memory. Otherwise, allocate new memory.\n    \"\"\"\n    if weight_decay == 0:\n        return device_grads\n\n    # Reuse device_grads memory if it was already allocated for maximize\n    if can_reuse_grads and maximize:\n        torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n        return device_grads\n    else:\n        return torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n            device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay\n        )\n\ndef _foreach_optimizer_step(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    step_fn: Callable[..., None],\n    state_tensors: dict[str, list[Optional[Tensor]]],\n    has_sparse_grad: bool = False,\n    maximize: bool = False,\n    weight_decay: float = 0.0,\n    lr: Union[float, Tensor] = 1e-3,\n    **kwargs: Any\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Shared foreach optimizer step with common preprocessing.\n\n    Args:\n        params: List of parameters to optimize\n        grads: List of gradients\n        step_fn: Callback function that implements optimizer-specific logic\n        state_tensors: Dict mapping state names to lists of state tensors\n        has_sparse_grad: Whether sparse gradients are possible\n        maximize: Whether to maximize loss instead of minimize\n        weight_decay: Weight decay factor\n        lr: Learning rate\n        **kwargs: Additional arguments passed to step_fn\n    \"\"\"\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    # Group all tensors by device and dtype\n    tensors_to_group = [params, grads] + list(state_tensors.values())\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        tensors_to_group,\n        with_indices=True,\n    )\n\n    for grouped_tensors_tuple, indices in grouped_tensors.values():\n        # Extract grouped tensors\n        device_params_ = grouped_tensors_tuple[0]\n        device_grads_ = grouped_tensors_tuple[1]\n        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n\n        # Extract state tensors\n        device_states = {}\n        for i, (state_name, state_list) in enumerate(state_tensors.items(), start=2):\n            device_states[state_name] = cast(list[Optional[Tensor]], grouped_tensors_tuple[i])\n\n        # Check for sparse gradients in this device group\n        device_has_sparse_grad = has_sparse_grad and any(\n            grad.is_sparse for grad in device_grads\n        )\n\n        # Apply maximize\n        device_grads = _foreach_apply_maximize(device_grads, maximize)\n\n        # Apply weight decay\n        device_grads = _foreach_apply_weight_decay(\n            device_grads, device_params, weight_decay, maximize\n        )\n\n        # Call optimizer-specific step function\n        step_fn(\n            device_params=device_params,\n            device_grads=device_grads,\n            device_states=device_states,\n            device_has_sparse_grad=device_has_sparse_grad,\n            indices=indices,\n            lr=lr,\n            **kwargs\n        )\n```\n\n### Step 2: Refactor SGD's `_multi_tensor_sgd`\n\nReplace the common preprocessing with `_foreach_optimizer_step` while keeping momentum-specific logic.\n\n### Step 3: Refactor Adam's `_multi_tensor_adam`\n\nApply same pattern, keeping Adam-specific logic (step increment, bias correction, AMSGrad).\n\n### Step 4: Update Other Optimizers\n\nIncrementally refactor Adagrad, RMSprop, RAdam to use shared helpers.\n\n## Key Design Decisions\n\n1. **Callback approach**: Keep optimizer-specific logic in a step_fn callback to avoid forcing all optimizers into the same mold\n2. **Memory efficiency**: Maintain the existing memory-reuse optimization (e.g., reusing device_grads when maximize is applied)\n3. **Gradual refactoring**: Don't require all optimizers to change at once; allow phased migration\n4. **Backward compatibility**: Existing public APIs unchanged; only internal _multi_tensor_* functions refactored\n5. **Type safety**: Use cast() appropriately and preserve existing type hints\n\n## Testing Strategy\n\n1. Run existing optimizer tests: `python -m pytest test/test_optim.py -xvs`\n2. Verify numerical equivalence between old and new implementations\n3. Check that state_dict serialization still works\n4. Ensure hook APIs still fire in correct order\n5. Validate LR scheduler integration\n\n## Success Criteria Verification\n\n- [ ] `/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py` created with shared logic\n- [ ] At least 3 optimizer files import from _foreach (SGD, Adam, Adagrad)\n- [ ] Duplicate foreach patterns reduced (code duplications removed)\n- [ ] Original optimizer APIs still work (all tests pass)\n- [ ] No performance regressions\n\n## Critical Files\n\n### To Create:\n- `/workspace/torch/optim/_foreach.py`\n\n### To Modify:\n- `/workspace/torch/optim/sgd.py` (lines 378-471 of _multi_tensor_sgd)\n- `/workspace/torch/optim/adam.py` (lines 544-787 of _multi_tensor_adam)\n- `/workspace/torch/optim/adagrad.py` (lines ~388-495 of _multi_tensor_adagrad)\n\n### Utilities to Import:\nFrom `torch.optim.optimizer`:\n- `_to_scalar`, `_get_value`, `_view_as_real`\n- `Optimizer`, `DeviceDict`, `DeviceDtypeDict`\n\n## Implementation Notes\n\n1. The _foreach_optimizer_step function signature is flexible to accept any state tensors\n2. Each optimizer's step_fn is responsible for its own logic (momentum, exponential moving averages, etc.)\n3. The shared function handles the \"mechanical\" parts: grouping, maximize, weight_decay\n4. Complex dtype handling will need special treatment - may need helper or documented pattern\n5. Sparse gradient handling varies by optimizer - kept in step_fn for now\n\n## Status: READY FOR IMPLEMENTATION\nPlan is complete and ready for execution. User approval requested.\n"}}],"fast_mode_state":"off","uuid":"b5249bef-6e56-463b-a0fc-e056fe08e8d3"}
